<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Calikang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4290)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Calikangs are giant, blue-skinned, six-armed guardians of ancient tombs and treasuries. They each feel a deep, inherent drive to protect and guard, making them highly sought after as wardens and bodyguards. Because most serve as solitary guardians, few calikang societies exist.

Calikangs' unique physiologies enable them to absorb and manipulate electrical magic as well as other energies. They can live for 200 years, though they can further extend their lives via suspended animation. For this reason, many are chosen to guard tombs or other sealed sites where living guardians would perish and constructs would deteriorate.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Calikang_Remastered.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Calikang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4290)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 66, [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 84

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +4

**Con** +5

**Int** -2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Suspended Animation** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) By concentrating for 5 minutes, the calikang can enter a state of suspended animation, freezing in place and becoming motionless but remaining aware of their surroundings. While in this state, the calikang gains a +4 status bonus to Fortitude saves, doesn't age, and is immune to [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) toxins, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [starvation, and thirst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603). A calikang can exit suspended animation as a free action. If they exit this state to attack, the calikang gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their initiative roll.

**Items**
_[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_ (2)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +20 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Skills.aspx?ID=27)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 235

</row>

**Immunities**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)

**Energy Conversion** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) Whenever the calikang is hit by an [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) spell's attack roll or rolls a successful save against a spell that deals electricity damage, they absorb the energy. This heals the calikang for an amount of HP equal to quadruple the spell's rank and recharges their Energy Breath. A calikang can't absorb their own spells this way.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
longsword +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d8+15 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 28
- **1st**
[Runic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658) (at will)
- **6th**
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462)
- **Constant (6th)**
[Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Energy Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The calikang exhales a blast of energy that deals 13d6 energy damage to creatures in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The calikang can choose the damage type each time: [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697). This ability gains the trait of the chosen damage type. Increase the die size to d8 if the calikang chooses electricity.

**Sixfold Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The calikang makes up to two longsword Strikes and up to four fist Strikes. Each Strike must be against a different target. These attacks count toward the calikang's multiple attack penalty, which doesn't increase until after all the attacks are complete. For 1 round, the calikang gains a circumstance bonus to their AC equal to the number of Strikes they choose not to take to a maximum of +4 for taking only two Strikes.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Calikang Origins</title>

Legend holds that an ancient Vudrani god failed to protect an important treasury from a raid by [asuras](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=571). In shame, he severed his fingers and cast them down upon the world. Calikangs arose from the fingers, and, as penance, they've sought to protect worldly holdings from robberies or invasions ever since.
</aside>
name: Calikang

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Camel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4291)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

For generations untold, nomads and traders have relied on sure-footed camels to cross the harsh deserts and trackless wastes around the world. Thriving where other animals wither and perish, camels are well-adapted to their homes with tough skin and the ability to store nutrients within their bodies. Properly cared for, these “ships of the desert” can trek for weeks between oases without trouble. 

Camels have three eyelids to protect them from desert sands and other blowing debris. One lid is completely clear, which allows them to see and travel during high winds. When sandstorms strike, camels completely close their nostrils to protect their lungs. Their underbellies also sport a thick, specialized skin, allowing them to lie down safely on burning hot sands. 

Contrary to popular belief, fatty tissue comprises a camel's humps rather than water. This stored energy allows the animals to survive long distances between feedings. These herbivores can also readily digest tough scrub brush inedible to other species, making them one of the hardiest desert survivors. Strong as warhorses, camels can run fast and even sprint for short periods of time when they feel threatened, though they prefer a slow, plodding pace to conserve energy. 

One-humped camels, also called dromedaries, are more common in the deserts of northern Garund, while the two-humped variety are native to the dry steppes of Casmaron. Both species have tall and lanky builds, standing about 6 feet at the shoulder and weighing around 2,000 pounds. They can be ornery when mishandled, and they don't hesitate to bite, kick, or even spit a noxious substance on riders who don't treat them well. 

In addition to transporting people and goods, camels are a key source of fiber for clothing and tents as well as milk. Their meat is highly nutritious and surprisingly tasty, but given the creatures' utility, this use is reserved for special occasions or truly dire situations. 

Camels are particularly common in the Golden Road region of the Inner Sea. Here, camel herders have developed a unique collection of sounds to communicate with their camels. An experienced camel herder can move entire flocks with a single sound, and some camels can react poorly to sounds they dislike.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Camel.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Camel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4291)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 67

**Perception** +4; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Desert-Adapted** A camel is well-adapted to heat and deserts. If allowed to drink and eat its fill (roughly 40 gallons), it can [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) for 2 weeks without needing to attempt [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks, and it treats environmental [heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) as if they were one step less severe.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +7,
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spit +6,
**Damage** camel spit

**Camel Spit**   To drive away enemies, the camel spits the partially digested contents of its stomach at a creature within 10 feet. On a hit, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round and must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. The camel can't use its camel spit Strike again for 1d4 rounds.

**Sand Stride** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The camel [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice. It has a +5-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed during these Strides, ignoring difficult terrain caused by rubble, sand, and uneven ground made of earth and stone.

   

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Camel Cousins</title>

Rumors in the high desert tell of an ancient species related to both camels and llamas that still lives in sheltered mountain valleys and along hidden rivers: the camelops. Larger and stronger than domesticated camels, camelops remain wild creatures. No living examples exist in captivity, though fables of their luxurious coats and indomitable endurance lead some riders to seek them out regardless.
</aside>
name: Camel

Never have legend and misinformation met upon a more inauspicious brow than that of the lowly carbuncle. At first glance, they appear to be little more than ungainly reptiles. What sets them apart are their strange magical abilities and the gemstone-like horn protruding from between their goggling eyes. Although rumors suggest various uses for carbuncle horns, ranging from miracle cure-alls to potent magical components, the truth is much more mundane: a carbuncle's horn is merely a highly reflective growth, not unlike a fingernail. Carbuncles also possess a strange sense that allows them to detect treasure that has been hidden or obscured. The creatures generally feel a compulsion to move toward these treasures and remain nearby. Strangely, carbuncles seem disinterested once the valuables are laid out in the open, as if the obfuscation were part of their interest. Many would-be treasure hunters end up following the creatures in hopes of finding treasures, though the fearfulness of carbuncles makes this a very difficult prospect. Most carbuncles abandon their nests after being scared away, even when their sense would typically draw them back. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Carbuncle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4292) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 68 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), treasure sense (imprecise) 500 feet **Languages** carbuncle empathy 30 feet **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +3, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +6 **Str** -3 **Dex** +0 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Carbuncle Empathy** The carbuncle can telepathically send mild feelings and sensations to nearby creatures. It can't use this ability to communicate in language or hinder a target, but it might convey a feeling of dread or the scent of food cooking nearby.
**Treasure Sense** The carbuncle can sense the presence and location of any object or grouping of objects worth at least 50 gp in total within 500 feet of it. The carbuncle's sense only functions if the treasure is within in a container or physically obscured, such as when buried underground. Objects worn on a person or left out in open air don't trigger the carbuncle's sense. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +3 **Will** +6 **HP** 20 **Easy to Influence** Any mental spell can affect a carbuncle, regardless of creature type limitations. Against a [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) spell, a carbuncle always gets an outcome one degree of success worse than it rolled on its saving throw.
**Fatal Faker** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** The carbuncle takes damage; **Effect** The carbuncle feigns death by teleporting away and leaving a replica of its corpse behind, creating a colorful flash of light and a croaking sound. The real carbuncle transports to a clear space within 30 feet that it can see and leaves a hollow shell behind. The fake body appears solid until it is touched, at which point it crumbles to dust. --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** jaws +5 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 18 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482) - **1st** [Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=1580) (at will) - **3rd** [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584) (at will; self only) **Specious Suggestion** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The carbuncle concentrates on a creature it can see and tries to manipulate that creature, imploring them to perform harmless, pointless, and usually embarrassing actions. The target must attempt a DC 18 Will save. The target then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.
This has the effects of [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) except that a critical success bolsters the target and grants them a +1 status bonus to Will saves for 1 hour, the duration on a failure is 1 round, and the duration on a critical failure is a 1 minute. The target can attempt a new save at the end of its turn each round to end the effect.
Carbuncle Chatter “A lizard with an apple-sized gem sticking out of its forehead? Wishful thinking!” “Carbuncles are real! I almost caught one, but even though it could barely walk, its magic allowed it to escape my clutches.” “Their mind control powers could make them useful familiars, but if you ever catch one, it dies of fright.” “My advice is to stay away from these pests. They drink your hopes and aspirations through their horns, leaving you empty except for bad luck and bellyaches.” “Planning a carbuncle hunt? You’d better leave your self-respect at home and be ready to emerge with fewer friends than you had at the start!”
name: Carbuncle

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Catoblepas](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4293)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A catoblepas is an aggressive beast at the best of times. Though it prefers swamps, it has been known to forage in plains and forests for short periods, leaving behind hunting grounds tainted by its foul breath and noxious waste that other predators and prey alike avoid for days or even weeks thereafter. The catoblepas bullies those creatures it believes are a match for it and eats everything weaker. 

A catoblepas is 15 feet long and weighs 2,200 pounds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Catoblepas.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Catoblepas](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4293)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 69

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +20

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +4

**Con** +6

**Int** -2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 30 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (plus [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +24 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 215

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)

**[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=63)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+13 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
antler +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d12+13 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning

**Poison Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The catoblepas breathes a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of horrid fumes, dealing 13d6 poison damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). The area of this cone is reduced to 30 feet underwater. Targets that fail their saving throw also become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The catoblepas can't use its Poison Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 32

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Catoblepas Nests</title>

A catoblepas den is an awful place indeed—a filthy nest composed of piles of decaying vegetation, half-finished or half-digested meals of rotting animals, never-quite-dry banks of mud, and tangles of thorny branches. Worse yet, the catoblepas’s stink infuses such a site, imposing the beast’s ungodly stench upon those who would explore and search it for treasure. A catoblepas nest can retain its stink in this way for up to a week after it’s been abandoned by its foul denizen.
</aside>
name: Catoblepas

Engineers, technologists, and mechanically gifted [wizards](/Classes.aspx?ID=39) employ clockwork spies—tiny, spiderlike constructs capable of recording and playing back audio—to surreptitiously surveil their enemies or steal secrets from competitors. Their spindly bodies and delicate components make them unsuitable for combat; in fact, most builders construct clockwork spies with a self-destruct mechanism to ensure the spies' meddling can't be traced back to them. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Clockwork Spy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4294) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 70 **Perception** +8; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +5 **Str** -1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Record Audio** The clockwork spy records all sounds within 25 feet onto a small gemstone worth 1 gp embedded in its body. The clockwork spy can record up to 1 hour of sound on a single gemstone. Once it begins recording, it can't cease recording early, nor can it record onto a gemstone that already contains a recording. Some clockwork spies contain multiple gemstones to allow for a series of recordings. Since clockwork spies are not intelligent, they must be given simple commands regarding when to start recording sounds. A clockwork spy can differentiate between different kinds of creatures but not between specific individuals.
The spy can start or stop playback of recorded sound by spending a single action. Removing a gemstone from or installing a gemstone into a clockwork spy requires a successful DC 14 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411); on a failure, the gemstone is undamaged, but any recorded sounds are erased and the gemstone still can't be used to make another recording.
**[Wind-Up](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=575)** 24 hours, DC 14, standby --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +2 **Ref** +7 **Will** +4 **HP** 7 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2315), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 2, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) 2 **Self-Destruct** A clockwork spy must use this reaction unless specifically programmed otherwise by its creator; **Trigger** The clockwork spy is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The spy thrashes around and emits a tinny scream followed by a steady ticking sound. At the beginning of what would have been its next turn, the clockwork spy explodes, dealing 1d10 piercing damage in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Its gemstone is destroyed, along with any information contained inside it.
An adjacent creature can cancel the self-destruct sequence by succeeding at a DC 16 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** spherical body +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6–1 bludgeoning
name: Clockwork Spy

A clockwork mage is a lethal blend of magic and machinery. Each of these clockworks is imbued with an arcane stone at its core that powers spells through the wand embedded in its chest. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Clockwork Mage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4296) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 72 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -5 **[Wind-Up](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=575)** 24 hours, DC 26, standby **Items** [clockwork wand](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2731) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 115 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2315), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919)) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) 10 **Clockwork Wand** The clockwork mage uses a mechanical wand as a focus to channel magical energy. This wand is built into the clockwork mage's chest, with only the crystal at the end exposed. The mage can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to the remove the wand, or someone else can remove it with a DC 31 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411). The clockwork mage becomes unable to cast any spells except cantrips while the wand is removed.
When removed, the clockwork wand is a magic wand containing the last 2nd-rank innate spell the clockwork mage cast (the GM determines the spell randomly if the mage has not cast any eligible spells). The spells are placed within the wand while the mage is created, and the creator can substitute other arcane spells of the appropriate rank. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+6 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **1st** [Carryall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1972), [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547) - **2nd** [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=2045) (Player Core 2 255) - **3rd** [Aqueous Orb](/Spells.aspx?ID=1443), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) - **4th** [Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) - **5th** [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676) **Energize Clockwork Wand** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The clockwork mage regains a spell it has already cast that day. It must spend 1 hour of its operational time, or 2 hours if the spell is 3rd rank or higher.
name: Clockwork Mage

markdown:
Clockwork dragons are marvels of clockwork design. These powerful masterpieces have the ability to fly, making them versatile and dangerous killers. A clockwork dragon's winding mechanism is more efficient than those of other clockworks, allowing it to partially reuse energy generated by flapping its wings. Creating a clockwork dragon requires not only an advanced understanding of clockwork design, but also a greater cache of rare resources, as its body requires solid reinforcement. As a result, most clockwork dragons have [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) fortification. Creating a clockwork dragon without adamantine is entirely possible, though such dragons are typically smaller and more fragile. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Clockwork Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4297) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 72 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -5 **[Wind-Up](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=575)** 1 week, DC 35, standby --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +28 **Will** +25 **HP** 265 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2315), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919)) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 15, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) 15 **Self-Destruct** A clockwork dragon must use this reaction unless specifically programmed otherwise by its creator; **Trigger** The clockwork dragon is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The dragon screeches to a stop and emits a steady, loud ticking sound. At the beginning of what would have been its next turn, the dragon explodes, dealing 12d10 piercing damage in a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
An adjacent creature can cancel the self-destruct sequence by succeeding at a DC 37 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411). --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** adamantine jaws +33 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing **Melee** adamantine claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Melee** tail +31 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning **Melee** wing +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+15 piercing **Breathe Oil** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Effect** The clockwork dragon breathes a spray of flaming oil that deals 16d6 fire damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) with a DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. Creatures that fail their saves are covered in burning oil and take 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The clockwork dragon can't Breathe Oil again for 2 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The clockwork dragon makes two adamantine claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Spearing Tail** The clockwork dragon attacks with the sharp point of its tail. It makes a tail Strike against each creature in a 20-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), rolling the attack roll once and applying the result to each target. Any creature that takes damage also takes 4d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (doubled on a critical hit). This counts as two attacks for the dragon's multiple attack penalty.
name: Clockwork Dragon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mix Coatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4298)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mix coatls (pronounced “meesh”) are guides to fledgling societies, providing fundamental information such as farming techniques, medicinal expertise, or more esoteric knowledge like the arcane arts. 

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Couatl_MixCouatl.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Mix Coatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4298)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
<trait label="Coatl" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=748" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 74

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** +4

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Star Child** The mix coatl is difficult to discern against starry skies. They can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) in the air at night without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or [concealment](/Rules.aspx?ID=424).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus mix coatl venom and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +18
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689)
- **1st**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (self only), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680)
- **3rd**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will)
- **4th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681)
- **6th**
[Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) (at will)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d10+4 bludgeoning, DC 26

**Gift of Knowledge**   When a mix coatl casts [_rewrite memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) on a willing creature, the mix coatl can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell to rewrite these memories for up to 60 continuous minutes. A mix coatl can grant knowledge of a particular skill to the target as part of the spell. The mix coatl chooses Engineering [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Farming Lore, Fishing Lore, Hunting Lore, or Mercantile Lore. The target becomes permanently trained in the chosen skill. A creature can benefit from Gift of Knowledge only once.

**Mix Coatl Venom**  ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) To [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures, this is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) instead of a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) and deals [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage instead of poison damage; **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage, [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1, and stupefied 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d8 poison damage, stunned 1, and stupefied 2 (1 round)

**Wrap in Coils** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The mix coatl has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in their jaws; **Effect** The mix coatl moves the creature into their coils, freeing their jaws to make attacks, and then uses Constrict against the creature. The mix coatl can hold as many creatures in their coils as will fit in their space.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-382" />
name: Mix Coatl

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Fiercer than other coatls, xiuh coatls (pronounced “shoo”) are dedicated to seeking out malevolent creatures and offering a final opportunity at redemption.

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +21, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** +5

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Thoughtsense** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The xiuh coatl senses all non-mindless creatures at the listed range.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)

[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 8

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus 2d8 fire, xiuh coatl venom, and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (self only; at will)
- **4th**
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524)
- **5th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)
- **6th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only)

**Rituals** DC 32

- **4th**
[Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108)

**[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d10+10 bludgeoning plus 1d8 electricity, DC 32

**Instrument of Retribution** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The xiuh coatl transforms into any simple or martial weapon. The weapon is always a _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [ shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847) weapon_ and can be of any size, chosen by the xiuh coatl when they transform. In weapon form, the xiuh coatl gains [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) equal to that of the weapon into which they transform, and they retain their Hit Points, saving throws, senses, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79), and traits while in weapon form. The xiuh coatl can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) to revert to their normal form.

**Volcanic Lightning Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The xiuh coatl breathes a gout of flame and lightning in an 80-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) that deals 5d8 electricity damage and 5d8 fire damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The xiuh coatl can't use Volcanic Lightning Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Wrap in Coils** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The xiuh coatl has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in their jaws; **Effect** The xiuh coatl moves the creature into their coils, freeing their jaws to make attacks, and then uses Constrict against the creature. The xiuh coatl can hold as many creatures in their coils as will fit in their space.

**Xiuh Coatl Venom**  ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) To [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures, this is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) instead of a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) and deals [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage instead of poison damage; **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d8 poison damage and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d10 poison damage, enfeebled 1, and –5-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d8 poison damage, enfeebled 2, and –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round)

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cockroaches are communal creatures, rarely setting out on their own unless they are searching for food. If an adventurer encounters a single giant cockroach while exploring, they had best take precautions against others, as there's likely an entire colony nearby.

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Cockroach Species</title>

Beyond the common giant cockroach, other flesh-eating cockroaches exist throughout the world. These variations include the giant hissing cockroach, the noxious venomroach, the huge spitting cockroach (which can incapacitate enemies from a distance), the aggressive sawback cockroach, and the mysterious and rare dragonroach.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cockroach Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4301)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While cockroaches tend to gather in cramped spaces, a disturbed colony is sometimes prone to swarming, where hundreds or even thousands of the insects scurry out of their hiding places in a raft of shiny brown and black carapaces on thousands of skittering legs. Given how disgusting many find cockroaches to be, encountering such a swarm can be alarming to even the most seasoned adventurers. The creatures are relentless when disturbed; in contrast to the insects' normally skittish and harmless nature, cockroach swarms pursue the creature or creatures that provoked them and harry them with thousands of stinging bites.

markdown:
These skittering creatures are prized for their delicious meat, but their size makes them dangerous targets for harvesting. The statistics presented here represent giant crabs that live close to the water's surface. Crabs that live deeper underwater often exhibit more extreme adaptations to their environment. For example, crabs living in the depths where little light reaches them gain darkvision and cold resistance, and those adapted to the most hostile reaches of the deep sea can detect nearby creatures through subtle shifts in ocean currents. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Giant Crab](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4302) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 77 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +9 **Will** +7 **HP** 25 **Resistances** physical 3 (except bludgeoning) **Vulnerable to Prone** If a creature critically succeeds at a check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) the giant crab, the crab is flipped over onto its back in addition to the usual effects. Until it Stands, a giant crab that's flipped onto its back has a particularly hard time defending itself; instead of taking the normal –2 circumstance penalty to AC for being [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), it takes a –4 circumstance penalty to AC.
**Scuttle** **Trigger** A creature that the giant crab can see targets the crab with an attack while the giant crab isn't prone; **Effect** The giant crab scuttles to the side and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. After the attack resolves, the crab can [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to its speed in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) as part of the reaction. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 15 feet **Melee** claw +9, **Damage** 1d10+4 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d6+3 bludgeoning, DC 18

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When hermit crabs find a shell that's too large, they lie in wait for others to arrive and move into the shell, abandoning a possible replacement. This can form a large chain of queuing crabs waiting to upgrade their shells. Interruptions can aggravate the crabs, resulting in attacks against the unfortunate source of disruption.

markdown:
Souls who die from neglect or exposure due to becoming lost, trapped in small spaces, or otherwise being far from help can transform into lacridaemons. These weaker daemons are full of despair and typically lash out against anyone they see, believing them to have intentionally abandoned the lacridaemon to their fate. Many lacridaemons are born from the souls of wicked mortals who push others away and thus suffer lonely deaths. Exiled, corrupt nobles and violent criminals left to rot in solitary prisons are common among their ranks. Lacridaemons tend to resemble humanoids with smooth, gray skin, a strange tail, and vicious claws. They each have patches of frost across their flesh, representing their ultimately cold and lonely ends. These patches appear on every lacridaemon even if their deaths weren't due to exposure to cold, as is the case for souls who perished alone from thirst in the desert. Even in their fiendish state, lacridaemons can't help but consider themselves alone. It's not uncommon to find groups of lacridaemons where each daemon acts independently, as if the others weren't there. They tend to sit alone in dark corners reminiscent of the places of they died, sobbing loudly, but once they become aware of the presence of non-fiends, they are quick to attack, believing these creatures to be the same ones who abandoned them in life. A lacridaemon's supernatural abilities allow the fiend to subject others to a fate similar to the their own death, making it difficult to escape danger and find help. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Lacridaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4304) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 78 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +12 **Will** +6 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 45 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Weeping Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 60 feet. The sounds of crying constantly surround a lacridaemon. A creature that first enters the area must attempt a DC 17 Will save as the sounds cause major disorientation. On a failure, the creature takes a –2 status penalty to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) (–4 on a critical failure) for 1 day. After attempting the save, the creature is temporarily immune to the lacridaemon's weeping aura for 1 day. The penalties from multiple weeping auras can increase up to a cumulative total of –10.
**Steal Bearings Trigger A creature within 30 feet Strides** Effect The lacridaemon attempts to redirect the triggering creature so it eventually becomes as lost as the lacridaemon. The triggering creature attempts a DC 17 Will save. Regardless of the result, the creature becomes temporarily immune to all attempts to Steal Bearings for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature treats all squares as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for its [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).
**Critical Failure** As failure, except that the lacridaemon determines where the target moves during the Stride, though it can't move it into [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2587) or a place it can't stand. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +12 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing plus 1d6 cold and lacridaemon venom **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d6+4 slashing plus 1d6 cold **Divine Innate Spells** DC 17 - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Vanishing Tracks](/Spells.aspx?ID=1737) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) **Lacridaemon Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d8 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), and stupefied 1 (1 round) **Venomous Spray** The lacridaemon's begins to weep, spraying its venom-filled tears at all creatures within 30 feet. The creatures are immediately exposed to lacridaemon venom. Other lacridaemons are immune to this venom.
Those Who Die Alone The most common souls that spawn lacridaemons are those of wicked individuals abandoned to lonely deaths. Dangerous, reclusive villains who meet their ends at the hands of adventurers in remote lairs tend to become lacridaemons. In addition, those who die exposed to the natural elements, be it from intense heat, freezing temperatures, or thirst, can end up as lacridaemons.
name: Lacridaemon

markdown:
Meladaemons personify death by starvation and thirst, and they revel in spreading the same despair that brought about their mortal demise. When they aren't blighting fields, massacring livestock, or tainting water supplies, they experiment on prisoners to study how long creatures can go without sustenance and the deleterious effects that result from such deprivation. Fiercely loyal to [Trelmarixian](/Deities.aspx?ID=393), Apocalypse Rider of Famine, they serve no other beings. They work alongside other daemons if Trelmarixian wills it but are notoriously traitorous. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Meladaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4305) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 79 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +20 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 225 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **Consumptive Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 20 feet. A meladaemon emanates an aura of intense hunger. Each round a creature begins its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 27 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d6 void damage (2d6 on a critical failure) and becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). This fatigue ends as soon as the creature eats any food.
**Withering Opportunity** **Trigger** The meladaemon is attacked by an adjacent creature, and the attack misses; **Effect** The meladaemon swipes at the triggering creature, which must immediately attempt a save against the meladaemon's withering touch. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d12+16 plus daemonic famine **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+16 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) and withering touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 31 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) (at will) - **6th** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) **Rituals** DC 31 - **4th** [Blight](/Rituals.aspx?ID=111) **Daemonic Famine** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** as stage 3; **Stage 5** enfeebled 3 (1 week); **Stage 6** dead **Withering Touch** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) When the meladaemon hits with a claw Strike or a creature begins its turn [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the meladaemon, the creature must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d6 void damage and becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). This fatigue ends when the creature drinks.
Formed In His Image Meladaemons have always been gaunt and bestial, but they didn't always resemble jackals. When [Trelmarixian](/Deities.aspx?ID=393) overthrew the previous Apocalypse Rider of Famine, one of his first acts as a ruler of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=37) was to forcibly twist the appearance of his deacon caste to resemble his own wicked form. He went on to imbue meladaemons with other jackal-like aspects as it suited him, further warping them and cementing their fealty.
name: Meladaemon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Thanadaemon (Death Daemon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4306)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Also known as the deacons of death, thanadaemons represent death via old age. They're among the most patient daemons and prefer to bide their time to enact far-reaching, decades-long plans rather than fight (though they're still deadly foes). Like the Apocalypse Rider of Death, they ply the waters of the River Styx in search of wayward souls. Rarely are thanadaemons seen without a trademark oar (which they wield as a bo staff), a tool they use to navigate the river's muck-ridden channels and turbulent rapids.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Daemon_Thanadaemon.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Thanadaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4306)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Daemon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=568" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 80

**Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22, [Styx Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +6

**Con** +4

**Int** +3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +7
</row>

**Items**
_[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)_, _soul gem_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +21 

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +26 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 270

</row>

**Immunities**
[death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331)

**Weaknesses**
[holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10

**Terrifying Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 30 Will save. If the creature fails, it becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. The creature is then temporarily immune to terrifying gaze (but not Focus Gaze) for 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_bo staff_ +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d8+16 bludgeoning plus 1d6 void and draining strike

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 3d6+17 slashing plus draining strike

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 34
- **4th**
[Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will)
- **5th**
[Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- **6th**
[Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) (×2)
- **7th**
[Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; self plus skiff and passengers only; Astral; Ethereal; and unholy planes only), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720)
- **Constant (7th)**
[Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Rituals** DC 34

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117)

**Draining Strike**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a thanadaemon damages a living creature with a melee Strike, the creature must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1. Further damage dealt by the thanadaemon increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4.

**Focus Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The thanadaemon glares at a single creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a DC 33 Will save against the thanadaemon's terrifying gaze. If the target was already [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), a failed save causes it to become [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1d4 rounds; this second effect has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait. After attempting its save, the creature is temporarily immune to this ability until the start of the thanadaemon's next turn.

**Soul Crush** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The thanadaemon has a soul gem; **Effect** The thanadaemon crushes the soul gem in one hand and gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 15 for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Styx Passage Fees</title>

Gliding with eerie ease along the murky River Styx, thanadaemons are only too happy to offer mortals passage aboard their dilapidated skiffs—for a price. Gold is typically an acceptable payment for a thanadaemon's services, though these fiends are well-known for altering the details of arrangements after the fact and may just as likely demand a favor or some esoteric good, such as a _soul gem_, instead of coin.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-386" />
name: Thanadaemon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sordesdaemon (Pollution Daemon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4307)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sordesdaemons are hulks of sewage and daemonic flesh that embody death through pollution. They are among the newest types of daemons to appear on Golarion and are constantly surrounded by a cloud of foul mist that chokes living creatures. Sordesdaemons are fiendishly clever and often seek to inspire mortals with new ideas and inventions that despoil the environment. While these daemons are more than capable of ruining habitats on their own, they take great delight in encouraging mortals to do so themselves, as the act can eventually create new sordesdaemons. Once a given forest, river or other natural abode is completely polluted, a sordesdaemon often moves to claim it as their domain.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Daemon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=568" />

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +28, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +28

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +3

**Con** +9

**Int** +6

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 37 

**Fort** +30 

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +26 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

[death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)

**Weaknesses**
[holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15

**Miasma of Pollution** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) 30 feet. A creature that enters the aura or begins its turn in it must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or be [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 (plus [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). Creatures in the aura can't reduce the value of the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute. Creatures made of water (such as [water elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=412)) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures use an outcome one degree of success worse than the result of their save.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +29 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus pollution infusion

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 38
- **4th**
[Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will)
- **5th**
[Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- **8th**
[Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), [Spiritual Epidemic](/Spells.aspx?ID=2030)

**Pollution Infusion**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=726)) Non-[fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) creatures adjacent to the afflicted creature take a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws against disease; **Saving Throw** DC 36 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 day); **Stage 2** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 and drained 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** doomed 1 and drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** doomed 2 and drained 2 (1 week); **Stage 5** dead

**Retch of Foulness** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The sordesdaemon exhales a spray of sewage that deals 8d6 acid damage and 8d6 poison damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). It can't use Retch of Foulness again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Daemonic Pollution</title>

Sordesdaemons who aren't compelled to pursue a specific task often find their way into sewers beneath large cities, where they subjugate other creatures that wallow in filth (such as ofalths). They think nothing of sacrificing these minions if it advances their own aims.
</aside>

markdown:
The horrifying phasmadaemons make use of illusions and their own frightening appearances (that of serpents with bony tails, horned crocodilian skulls for heads, and several insectile pincers) to strike fear into the hearts of others. They represent deaths brought about by fright. Ultimately, any kind of fear can lead to the creation of a phasmadaemon, but a soul who transforms into such a fiend must quickly find other fears to feed on to build up their own strength. Without the nourishment of human fear, the newborn phasmadaemon begins to waste away, eventually “dying” and becoming planar quintessence. Only the most clever and capable phasmadaemons survive their initial hunger, learning how to prey on even the bravest souls as a result. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Phasmadaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4308) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 81 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +29 **Str** +8 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +26 **Will** +31 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 340 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 35
**Unending Terror** Escaping fear near a phasmadaemon is no simple task. Creatures in the phasmadaemon's frightful presence aura don't reduce the value of their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition automatically at the end of their turns. Instead, they must attempt a Will save at the end of their turn against the DC of the effect that caused the condition. On a success, the creature's frightened condition is reduced by 1. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d10+19 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+19 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) (at will) - **5th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) (×2), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **6th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) (×2) - **7th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) - **8th** [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595) - **9th** [Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1505), [Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629) - **Constant (9th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 3d10+9 bludgeoning, DC 35 **Consume Fear** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The phasmadaemon has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); Effect The phasmadaemon feeds on the creature's mortality and innate terror, dealing 6d8 mental damage. The creature must attempt a DC 38 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage and manages to break free from the phasmadaemon's Grab.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and increases their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) conditioned by 1, to a maximum of frightened 4.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and increases their frightened condition by 2, to a maximum of frightened 4. If the creature is already frightened 4, it must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude saving throw. If it fails, it's reduced to 0 Hit Points and dies. This effect has the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) traits. **Inescapable Form** The phasmadaemon can [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) through tight spaces as if it were a Small creature. While Squeezing, it can move at its full Speed. The phasmadaemon can even Squeeze through spaces that typically fit only a Tiny creature but does so at the standard speed for Squeezing. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw
Fearful Machinations While daemons don't typically require food, phasmadaemons seem intent on feeding on the emotions, and especially the fears, of mortals. Some believe that the life force and emotions of creatures serves as the fuel for a phasmadaemon's magic, while others suspect that phasmadaemons simply enjoy facing mortals eye-to-eye while feeding, delighting in the fear of their prey's final moments.
name: Phasmadaemon

markdown:
While war can have many facets, obcisidaemons care only for the brutality and violence that conflict brings. These devastating daemons seek only to destroy, leaving rubble and ash in their wake. Reflecting the mortal desire to fully eradicate one's enemy, obcisidaemons never intentionally leave a soul behind in their war paths. They claim the souls of those they slaughter, adding them to a profane cloak that seeps from their bodies as a clinging mist. Souls added to the cloak are unable to travel into the afterlife, and most obcisidaemons can hold only a handful of these souls at a time. Whenever an obcisidaemon's cloak is overburdened or during times when the fiend needs greater power, they consume a soul and receive rejuvenation or the might they need to cause further destruction. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Obcisidaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4309) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 82 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +39, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +32, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36 **Str** +10 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 **Items** _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=381)_ --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +32 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 425 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 20 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Scorched Earth** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 60 feet. Any creature that dies within the aura and isn't drawn into the obcisidaemon's cloak of souls via Inherit Soul must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature's body (but not its gear) is immediately reduced to a fine smear of ashes. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d10+23 piercing **Melee** jaws +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d6+20 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **6th** [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) - **7th** [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Spell Riposte](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027) - **9th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492), [Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521) - **10th** [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596) - **Constant (10th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Consume Soul** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The obcisidaemon consumes a soul from their cloak to gain one of the following effects. A soul consumed in this way can't be resurrected except by a wish ritual or a similarly powerful effect.
_Empower Spell_ The obcisidaemon gains a +2 status bonus to their spell DCs and spell attack modifiers until the end of their next turn.
_Empower Weapon_ The obcisidaemon's weapon gains the effects of a [_greater flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), [_greater frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), [_greater shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847), or [_wounding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) rune until the end of their next turn.
_Healing_ ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) The daemon regains 8d8+64 Hit Points. **Inherit Soul** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) **Trigger** The obcisidaemon slays a creature; **Effect** The obcisidaemon attempts to draw the creature's soul into their cloak of souls. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, its soul is consumed and added to the cloak of souls. If the obcisidaemon's cloak can't hold any more souls, the daemon can release one of the souls as a free action; otherwise, the soul isn't absorbed.
Soul Hoarding Obcisidaemons carry some souls for months or even years at a time, choosing never to consume them even when doing so might grant them an advantage in combat.
name: Obcisidaemon
