markdown:
Vanyvers are large humanoid bats shaped from void and shadowstuff, their red eyes glowing like tiny stars in an otherwise lightless night. Though powerful, they're the least of the darvakkas and the most likely to submit to a master—either to another of their kind or to a mortal with a reputation for being especially murderous and destructive. Vanyvers agree to follow their masters as a temporary means of maximizing the death and destruction they enact, but they will quickly turn on anyone they feel falters in this goal. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Vanyver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4310) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 84 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision,](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Void Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +23 **Will** +22 **HP** 275 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10 **Entropy's Shadow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 40 feet. Darvakkas leak entropy and corruption from their very being. A living creature entering or starting its turn in the aura takes 3d6 void damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. If it fails, it's also [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute and pulled 10 feet toward the darvakka.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** A darvakka caught in sunlight is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 as long as it remains in the sunlight.
**Catching Bite** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the vanyver's jaws makes a melee Strike against the vanyver with a weapon; **Effect** The vanyver chooses to be hit. If the attack would've missed, it hits. The vanyver catches the weapon in their jaws and uses Drain Magic on it without fulfilling Drain Magic's requirements. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 piercing plus 1d10 cold and Drain Magic **Melee** talon +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus 1d10 cold and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** wing +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+11 bludgeoning plus 1d10 cold **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26); [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28); or [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) only) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57) l 3d10+5 piercing, DC 33** **Drain Magic** **Requirements** The vanyver's last action was a successful jaws [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a creature, object, or spell effect; **Effect** The vanyver casts an innate [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) on the same target; if the target was a creature, the vanyver can target a spell affecting the creature instead. If a spell effect or item is successfully [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280), the vanyver gains temporary Hit Points equal to double the counteract rank of the effect that was counteracted. **Snatch** The vanyver can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at half Speed while they have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in either or both of their talons, carrying that creature along with them.
name: Vanyver

markdown:
The most common darvakkas are sykevers, dauntless warriors who can march on two limbs or four. Their forelimbs end in curved, excruciatingly sharp spikes instead of hooves or hands. Bloodthirsty but calculating, they lead legions of dead into battle in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), working toward the simple goal of ending all life. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Sykever](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4311) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 85 **Perception** +29; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +28, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27, [Void Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +25 **Will** +31 **HP** 310 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10 **Entropy's Shadow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 40 feet. Darvakkas leak entropy and corruption from their very being. A living creature entering or starting its turn in the aura takes 4d6 void damage with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. If it fails, it's also [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute and pulled 10 feet toward the darvakka.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** A darvakka caught in sunlight is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 as long as it remains in the sunlight. --- **Speed** 40 feet; [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) **Melee** horn +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+12 bludgeoning plus 1d10 cold and 2d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Melee** arm spike +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+12 piercing plus 1d10 cold **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×3) - **6th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **7th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26); [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28); or [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) only), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) - **Constant (8th)** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) **Change Posture** The sykever changes between their bipedal and quadrupedal stance. In their bipedal stance, the sykever can use all the abilities in their stat block except Horned Rush. In their quadrupedal stance, the sykever has a Speed of 80 feet but can't make arm spike Strikes, [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), cast spells, or use Crush Item. **Crush Item** **Trigger** The sykever gets a critical success to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383); **Requirements** The sykever is in their bipedal stance; **Effect** The sykever snatches the item and pierces it with their arm spikes. The item becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) and falls to the ground in the sykever's space. Items that are already broken aren't further damaged, and an item with 14 or higher [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) is unaffected. **Draining Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The sykever fixes their nightmarish gaze on one creature they can see, who must attempt a DC 36 Will save. Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 for 1 round if the sykever is in bipedal stance or clumsy 2 for 1 round if the sykever is in quadrupedal stance.
**Failure** As success, but the effect lasts 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but enfeebled 3 or [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 3, and the effect lasts 10 minutes. **Horned Rush** **Requirements** The sykever is in their quadrupedal stance; **Effect** The sykever Strides and then makes a horn Strike.
The Bound One Hidden beneath the necromantic colleges of Yled in the nation of Geb are a trio of sykevers held in magical stasis alongside an ancient darvakka known only as the Bound One. This creature, ensnared by Geb himself, serves as an unending pool of void energy, immensely useful for magical experiments and empowering other undead servitors around the nation. The four darvakkas await the day Geb calls upon them once more.
name: Sykever

markdown:
An urveth is a massive, four-armed burrowing terror with a wormlike body and gaping maw that devours everything it can. Urveths burrow deep underground to hide from the sun, emerging under the cover of darkness to kill and consume. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Urveth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4312) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 86 **Perception** +32; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +35, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +31, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +31, [Void Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +31 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +29 **Will** +34 **HP** 460 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=60), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 15 **Entropy's Shadow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 60 feet. Darvakkas leak entropy and corruption from their very being. A living creature entering or starting its turn in the aura takes 5d6 void damage with a DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. If it fails, it's also [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute and pulled 10 feet toward the darvakka.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** A darvakka caught in sunlight is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 as long as it remains in the sunlight.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** claw only. An urveth gains 3 extra reactions each round that they can use only to make Reactive Strikes. --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 60 feet, [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) **Melee** jaws +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+14 slashing plus 2d10 cold and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** claw +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus 2d10 cold **Melee** stinger +36 ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+14 piercing plus 2d10 cold and urveth venom **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (at will) - **8th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3), [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26); [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28); or [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) only), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **Constant (9th)** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) **Frenzy** The urveth makes two claw Strikes and one stinger Strike in any order. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Huge, 2d10+5 bludgeoning, Rupture 35. A living creature that ends its turn swallowed whole by an urveth becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 or increases its drained condition by 1, and the urveth gains 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). A creature whose drained condition increases to 5 in this way dies. **Urveth Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) Saving Throw DC 37 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 void damage and 2d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 void damage, 2d6 poison damage, and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 void damage, 2d6 poison damage, and enfeebled 4 (1 round)
name: Urveth

markdown:
Nasurgeths are hungry voids with glowing eyestalks and thousands of teeth. They lurk deep beneath the waves where the sunlight doesn't reach. At night, they ascend to the skies and rain destruction and ruin on the living. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Nasurgeth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4313) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 87 **Perception** +36; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +39, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +36, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +34, [Void Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36 **Str** +11 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +8 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +32 **Will** +36 **HP** 510 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 15 **Midnight Depths** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 60 feet. A nasurgeth's entropy grows even stronger underwater. All water within the aura is completely dark (as 4th-rank [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480)). Magical light with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank of 4th or lower and magical light cantrips are suppressed. A living creature entering or starting its turn in the aura takes 4d6 void damage, and the creature also takes an additional 2d10 cold damage if it's in water (DC 39 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). If it fails, it's also [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute and pulled 10 feet toward the nasurgeth.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** A darvakka caught in sunlight is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 as long as it remains in the sunlight.
**Spray Black Bile** **Trigger** The nasurgeth takes slashing or piercing damage from a critical hit, or a swallowed creature cuts itself free; **Effect** Darkness and death energy spill out from the nasurgeth's wound, dealing 8d8 void damage to creatures in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. --- **Speed** fly 60 feet, swim 80 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+19 piercing plus 2d10 cold and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+19 bludgeoning plus 2d10 cold **Divine Innate Spells** DC 43 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26); [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28); or [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) only), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **8th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508) (×3), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3) **Broken Barb** **Requirements** A creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in the nasurgeth's jaws; **Effect** The nasurgeth breaks a tooth off in the target, who takes 3d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and is no longer grabbed or restrained. If the target is adjacent to a surface, the tooth also pins it in place, making it immobilized ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 45). **Ravenous Void** The nasurgeth barrels forward with their mouth open, [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) twice in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) and moving through the spaces of Huge or smaller creatures. The nasurgeth deals the damage of their jaws Strike to each creature whose space they enter (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Any creature that critically fails is automatically [Swallowed Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77). **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Huge, 2d10+9 bludgeoning, Rupture 40. A living creature that ends its turn swallowed whole by a nasurgeth becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 or increases its drained condition by 1, and the nasurgeth gains 20 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 10 minutes . A creature whose drained condition increases to 5 in this way dies.
name: Nasurgeth

An average mature deep one weighs 300 pounds and measures 7 feet in length, though they appear shorter on land due to their wide stance and natural hunch. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Deep One](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4314) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 88 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) (precise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +6, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +5, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +4, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +4 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Pressurized** A deep one is immune to damage and other negative effects from changes in water pressure. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +4 **Will** +8 **HP** 24 **Immunities** endless **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 2, piercing 3 **Endless** A deep one doesn't age and is immune to spells and other effects that inflict magical aging. Unless killed, a deep one lives forever. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** jaws +7, **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **Melee** claw +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d4+3 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 14, attack +6 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482) - **1st** [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561) **Fervent Frenzy** The deep one makes two claw Strikes and one jaws Strike in any order. If the target creature is currently [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) by a deep one's Share Devotion ability, it's [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against these attacks. The deep one becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the start of their next turn. **Share Devotion** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The deep one fills their enemies' minds with terrible hallucinations of the Outer Gods. All enemy creatures within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 17 Will save; regardless of the result, a creature is temporarily immune to Share Devotion for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for as long as it's frightened.
name: Deep One

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Deep One Hybrid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4315)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Whether from their devout faith to [Dagon](/Deities.aspx?ID=429) or their own biological lineage, some humans display physical indicators of their connection to deep ones.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Deep_One_Hybrid.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Deep One Hybrid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4315)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 88

**Perception** +9; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +4, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +4, [Dagon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +5, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +4

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Natural Swimmer** The deep one hybrid can hold their breath underwater for 10 minutes and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [initiative](/Rules.aspx?ID=3300) rolls and Reflex saves while [swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381).

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358), [Fishing Tackle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2722), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41), [religious symbol of Dagon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745), [religious text of Dagon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2746), [Rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2748) (50 feet), [Trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +5 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Resistances**
piercing 2

**Ocean's Call** A deep one hybrid that remains 10 or more miles from the sea for 24 hours becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1. This effect is removed only once the hybrid returns to the sea.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
trident +4 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d8+1 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +4 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +4 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Divine Prepared Spells** DC 17, attack +9
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Haunting Hymn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×4), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554)

**Cleric Domain Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 17
- **1st**
[Tidal Surge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1848)

</column>

Given enough time, deep one elders can swell to dizzying heights. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Deep One Elder](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4316) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 89 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) 120 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +31, [Dagon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Ocean Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +25 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** +6 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Pressurized** A deep one elder is immune to damage and other negative effects from changes in water pressure. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +22 **Will** +26 **HP** 260 , regeneration [](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) 10 (deactivated by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 10, piercing 15 **Endless** A deep one elder doesn't age and is immune to spells and other effects that inflict magical aging. Unless killed, a deep one elder lives forever.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 31. A creature that fails its save is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure).
**Mental Mirror** [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects that fail against a deep one elder are reflected back onto the source, as [_spell riposte_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027). --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** foot +29 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d10+12 slashing plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) and devastation **Occult Innate Spells** DC 31, attack +23 - **4th** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **5th** [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) - **6th** [Blinding Fury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1968), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630) - **7th** [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) (×3) **Devastation** A deep one elder's claw Strikes ignore the first 10 [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) of an object. Additionally, on a critical hit, the target must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 31. The deep one can [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) up to double its swim Speed instead of [Striding](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). **Watery Void** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The deep one elder makes an endless void of water appear in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet, dragging creatures down into its whirlpool. If cast underwater, the watery void fills a 60-foot-tall cylinder with a 20-foot radius. Creatures in the area when the void appears and creatures that end their turn in the area take 3d8 bludgeoning damage and 3d8 void damage and must attempt a DC 31 Reflex save. The void remains until the end of the deep one elder's next turn. The deep one elder can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the void to extend the duration by 1 round, up to a total of 4 rounds, and can move the void up to 15 feet. Once the effect ends, the elder can't use Watery Void again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and a –5-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds while in the void.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds while in the void.
**Critical Success** The creature takes double damage, is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds while in the void.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vermlek (Worm Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4317)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Vermleks, also called worm demons, manifest from the souls of mortals who desecrated the dead, such as grave robbers or necromancers. These fiends take their violations to a new extreme, using their demonic powers to horrifically core their living victims and don the flesh husk that remains. 

In their natural form, a vermlek resembles an oversized pinkish worm with four long tails that end in writhing fibrils. Their mouth splits into four segments like a profane tulip lined with rows of dozens of pointed teeth. However, these fiends are often encountered only after they have crawled into the body of a dead humanoid and made the hollowed-out flesh their temporary host. Vermleks use their powers of deception and disguise to infiltrate mortal settlements and influence unwitting acquaintances of the bodies they wear. Particularly intelligent or conniving vermleks might even reach stations of real power within the ranks of an army or government, at which point exposure of their true form can wreak havoc among the populace they have so thoroughly duped.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vermlek.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Vermlek](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4317)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=573" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 90

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
[Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 (19 while Inhabiting a Body)

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 55

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5, [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 5

**Recoil from Wasted Opportunities** Vermleks can't stand the sight of a good meal presented and then swiftly taken away. Whenever a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature within sight of the vermlek has its dying condition removed, the vermlek takes 1d6 mental damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +12 ([Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d8+3 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
longsword +12 ([Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d8+4 slashing

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 19
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552)

**Abandon Body** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The vermlek is Inhabiting a Body; **Effect** The vermlek crawls out of the body they are inhabiting, devouring much of the body's remaining flesh and regaining 10 Hit Points in the process. The corpse they leave behind is little more than a husk.

**Inhabit Body** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The vermlek isn't already Inhabiting a Body; **Effect** The vermlek crawls into the body of an adjacent Medium humanoid that has been dead for no more than 1 week, consuming the bulk of the victim's skeleton and internal organs as they do so and cramming themself into the cavity. As long as they Inhabit a Body, the vermlek loses their bite attack, can wield weapons like a humanoid, gains a +3 circumstance bonus to AC, and gains a +3 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to Impersonate the creature they are inhabiting.

**Unsettling Movement**  ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) Whenever the vermlek Abandons a Body or Inhabits a Body, all creatures within 30 feet who can see the vermlek must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. On a critical failure, the creature is frightened 1 and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. Regardless of the result, creatures are immune to the same vermlek's unsettling movement for 24 hours.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Telltale Desecration</title>

Coroners and priests in demon-infested lands know immediately that they're dealing with a vermlek when they're brought a corpse that is little more than a sack of skin.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Abrikandilu (Wrecker Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4318)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wrecker demons, also known as abrikandilus, despise beautiful things and do everything in their power to destroy both people and objects regarded as such. An abrikandilu forms from the souls of mortals who were vandals, misanthropes, or defacers of artwork, particularly if their destructive actions stemmed from feelings of powerful envy.

An abrikandilu loathes only one thing more than beauty: their own visage. The mere sight of their face—reflected in a mirror, a shield, or even a pool of water—can send a wrecker demon into a rage. Many demon slayers leverage this to their advantage, venturing into battle with polished steel shields and cold iron blades at the ready.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Abrikandilu_Remastered.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Hatred of Mirrors** An abrikandilu loathes the sight of their reflection. When a creature [Interacts](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with a mirror within sight of the wrecker demon, the demon takes a –2 penalty to Will saves against [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks. An abrikandilu that ends their turn adjacent to a mirror or that's attacked by a creature holding a mirror takes 1d6 mental damage (this usually leads abrikandilus to focus their efforts on destroying nearby mirrors using Wreck).

</column>

**Loathsome Bite**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) When an abrikandilu hits a creature with their jaws Strike, the creature becomes infected with the demon's self-loathing. The creature must succeed at a DC 21 Will save to avoid gaining a –1 status penalty to Charisma-based checks. This penalty is cumulative up to –3, and remains even if the wounds are healed. The penalty is reduced by 1 every 24 hours until it reaches 0.

**Wreck** <actions string="Single Action" />  The abrikandilu makes two claw Strikes against an unattended object or held mirror. Held mirrors use the holding character's AC. If both Strikes hit, combine their damage for the purpose of overcoming any Hardness or resistance. These Strikes don't count toward the abrikandilu's multiple attack penalty, nor does that penalty apply to these Strikes.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vloriak (Despoiler Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4319)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Vloriaks rise from the sinful souls of those whose acts of sabotage resulted in despair and terror, and they delight in the destruction of mortal artifice. They make use of their ability to rust metals to destroy structures, precious works of art, and tools used for creation. In combat, this ability allows them to destroy the armaments of their attacks. Vloriaks particularly enjoy destroying armors and shields, as these defensive items generally bear elegant designs and decorations that are more enjoyable to despoil than those of a weapon. 

Often called despoiler demons, vloriaks were the followers of the longdead demon lord Xar-Azmak. Their numbers have dwindled over the eons since the Lord of Rust's death; those that exist now are scattered throughout [the Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45). Their chitinous bodies and faceted eyes give them an insectile appearance.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Vloriak.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Vloriak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4319)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=573" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 91

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** -1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +13 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Resistances**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 5

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5

**Restoration Vulnerability** A vloriak feels agonizing pain when a creature or object recovers from a debilitating effect in their proximity. The first time in a round in which a creature that is within sight of the demon reduces the value of their [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the demon takes 3d6 mental damage and cannot Lick Rust on their next turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +15 ([Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d6+6 slashing plus 1d6 spirit

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d6+2 acid plus 1d6 spirit and rust

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Caustic Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461)
- **2nd**
[Acid Grip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436) (×3), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670)
- **3rd**
[Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622)

**Lick Rust** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Requirements** The vloriak rusted a metal item with their tongue this turn; **Effect** The vloriak attempts a tongue [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) on the same target they just attacked. If it hits, it deals no damage as the demon licks away the rust and regains 2d6 Hit Points (or 4d6 Hit Points if the Strike was a critical hit). The vloriak can't Lick Rust on their next turn.

**Rust**   A vloriak's saliva causes metal to rust rapidly. If they succeed at a tongue [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) attempt, the vloriak deals 2d6 acid damage (doubled on a critical hit) to a metal item the target is wearing or holding, ignoring its [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183). If the vloriak hits an unattended metal item, the item takes this damage automatically. If a creature uses the [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75) reaction with a metal shield against a tongue attack, the shield is automatically [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), but no other item is rusted on that attack.

**Spew Rusted Shards** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The vloriak spews a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of acid and rusted metal. Creatures in the area take 3d6 acid and 3d6 piercing damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that takes any piercing damage is exposed to [tetanus](/Diseases.aspx?ID=28). The vloriak can't Spew Rusted Shards for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Vlorian Influence</title>

Vloriaks hail from the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) realm of Vlorus, and as such, carry within themselves the potential for decay and rusting ruin. Other creatures that dwell in Vlorus—particularly fiends who rise to power there—can gain similar powers over rust as well. [Qlippoth](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=460) are especially suited to this realm.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-387" />
name: Vloriak

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kithangian (Beast Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4320)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The kithangian—a horrifying amalgam of horse and scorpion also known as a beast demon—is a foul fiend born of the souls of mortals who abused and tormented animals in life. Kithangians tend to roam wild areas rife with animals to torture, though humanoid settlements with plenty of pets or livestock also make for tantalizing hunting grounds. Uncontested for too long, a kithangian's presence has a corrupting influence on the local fauna, which birth fiendish monstrosities until the demon is vanquished. If they realize they're being tracked by vengeful druids or demon slayers, a kithangian assumes the shape of an unassuming animal so they can get the jump on their pursuers. If a kithangian believes it can't win in a direct fight, it will disguise itself as an animal to flee the area and seek another place to continue its assault on a new batch of local animals.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Kithangian.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Kithangian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4320)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=573" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 92, [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 253

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680), [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +5

**Int** -2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 ([all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) (page 360))

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +19 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 180

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10

**Animal Kindness** Vulnerability Kithangians find kindness to animals revolting. The first time each round that a kithangian sees someone heal or otherwise provide aid to a creature that has the animal trait, the kithangian takes 3d6 mental damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pincer +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d12+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stinger +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus kithangian venom

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 25
- **1st**
[Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470) (at will; animals only)
- **3rd**
[Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) (×2)
- **4th**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- **Constant (4th)**
[Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680)

**Divine Rituals** DC 25

- **1st**
[Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142)

**Animal Killer**    A kithangian's melee Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to animals.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The kithangian transforms into a Medium or Large animal. This doesn't affect their statistics, but it may change the damage type of their Strikes.

**Kithangian Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage (2 rounds); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (2 rounds); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (2 rounds)

**Rasping Tongues** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The kithangian has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in one or both pincers; **Effect** Barbed tongues slither out of the faces in the kithangian's pincers. The tongues burrow into grabbed creatures and inject their minds with haunting psychic screams. Each grabbed creature takes 2d8 piercing damage and 2d8 mental damage. A creature can try to resist the mental damage by attempting a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Demon Hunters</title>

Reclaimers of the Sarkoris Scar who follow the [Green Faith](/Deities.aspx?ID=298) consider kithangians particularly heinous foes. They take grim satisfaction in hunting and slaying such demons, viewing their extermination as just vengeance for the evils these demons visit upon wildlife.
</aside>

<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Demon Hunters</title>

</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Urglid (Grave Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4321)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The hulking monstrosities known as urglids form from the souls of murderous undertakers, sadists who buried their victims alive, and intentionally neglectful grave keepers who gave up their watch over the dead. They seek to perpetuate their mortal sins as demons and use their fiendish powers to subject mortals to the terrors of vivisepulture. Other urglids find grave sites repulsive and will often destroy headstones and other grave markers. Without such markers, these graves are often lost and forgotten, a detail that pleases an urglid. Standing over 12 feet tall and weighing over 3,000 pounds, the demon has a head that seems sunken into their torso, and a gaping, toothy mouth that opens at the top of their chest.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Urglid.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Urglid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4321)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=573" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 94

**Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 60 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +27, [Outer Rifts Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +4

**Con** +5

**Int** +4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Consecration Vulnerability** Dedicated to the desecration of graves, an urglid takes 3d6+6 mental damage each round they're within the area of an effect with the [consecration](/Traits.aspx?ID=562) trait. In addition, the demon's [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) increases to 30 for 1 round the first time they take damage from [holy water](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3001) each turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +20 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 290

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 40 feet, climb 20 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 30
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) (at will)
- **5th**
[Magic Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1591) (at will), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751) (×3)
- **8th**
[Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507)
- **Constant (6th)**
[Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Divine Rituals** DC 32

- **1st**
[Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142)

**Earth Glide**   The urglid can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through any earthen matter, including rock. When they do so, the urglid moves at their full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing unless they choose to do so.

**Gravechoke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) The urglid emits a putrid pulse that targets all living creatures within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Each creature in this area that fails a DC 30 Fortitude save becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure).

**Ravenous Earth** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) With a single, devious thought, the urglid causes a mound of grave soil to well up at a creature's feet. That creature must succeed at a DC 30 Reflex save or become [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 30). The restrained creature then begins sinking below the ground into a spontaneously formed grave. A creature restrained by this ability for 3 rounds is buried 6 feet deep in the ground and begins suffocating within 1 minute. A buried creature must be dug up to be freed (see [Burial](/Rules.aspx?ID=2825)). A creature that is slain by Ravenous Earth rises as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=419) the next midnight.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Kabriri's Excavators</title>

While many loathe urglids for their ravenous appetite for burial and wanton destruction, priests of [Kabriri](/Deities.aspx?ID=432) insist that the demon lord blessed and oversaw their creation, trusting the fiends with digging the labyrinthine network of tunnels that connects Everglut with [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). It's no surprise, then, that where there's an urglid, there's possibly a pathway to the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45)—and plenty of ghouls.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-387" />
name: Urglid

markdown:
Born of the souls of doomsayers and cult leaders who sought to undermine and unravel reality, katpaskirs are demons of nihilism and unmaking. They pry into the edges of reality, placing themselves into the cracks between realms and scratching away at the composure of existence itself. Katpaskirs have an uncanny sense for locating portals and other planar convergences. They seek to corrupt and expand these gateways, setting them loose to expand on their own or drift across the multiverse. As they do, they slowly contribute to the ultimate unraveling of reality. Katpaskirs often appear as insectile humanoids with multiple arms or legs, each capable of scratching and clawing away at the seams of existence. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Katpaskir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4322) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 94 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), warp sense **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +31, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +31, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +31, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +31 **Str** +6 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +7 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Warp Sense** The katpaskir senses changes in the planar fabric within 1 mile, including any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effects, sensing the direction and distance to the disturbance. If it senses such a disturbance within 500 feet, the katpaskir can cast [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661) to observe the area without needing [line of sight](/Rules.aspx?ID=2383) to the location. --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +30 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 415 **Immunities** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 15, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Distortion Field** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 30 feet. Reality bends and warps all senses without displacing the katpaskir's actual location. Creatures of the katpaskir's choice who start their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or treat the area as [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) (DC 20). A creature that succeeds still treats the area as difficult terrain. For chosen creatures, the distance through the aura is doubled for determining range penalty.
**Breach Planar Wards** **Trigger** An effect attempts to prevent the katpaskir from using a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effect or from casting [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1701); **Effect** The katpaskir attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the triggering effect (counteract modifier of +29). The katpaskir automatically fails against an artifact's effect.
**Mirrored Summons** **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet that the katpaskir is aware of casts [_summon celestial_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1695) or otherwise summons a [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creature; **Effect** The katpaskir casts [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1701), regaining the daily ability to do so if needed. This effect is automatically [sustained](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) as long as the triggering summoning is sustained, for up to 1 minute. --- **Speed** 35 feet, burrow 15 feet, fly 35 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** claw +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d12+14 slashing plus 1d6 spirit **Melee** talon +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus 1d6 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **4th** [Planar Tether](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636) (at will) - **5th** [Scouting Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) - **8th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448) (×3), [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492), [Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532) - **9th** [Summon Fiend](/Spells.aspx?ID=1701) (demons only) - **Constant (9th)** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Rituals** DC 40 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Dimensional Ambush** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The katpaskir casts [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), then makes a melee Strike that deals three extra dice of damage. This Strike counts as two attacks when calculating the katpaskir's multiple attack penalty.
Rift Makers Katpaskirs sense weaknesses in planar fabric. These demons worm their way into spaces between dimensions and break down the barriers. They leave holes aimed at causing chaos and dissolution. In this way, katpaskirs gnaw at reality, hoping to one day unravel it.
name: Katpaskir

markdown:
Travelers and raiders from the cold, windswept dimension of Leng, these malevolent human-shaped creatures range across reality in ominous black ships capable of sailing beyond the borders of dimensions and planes. Although most denizens of Leng seek conquest and riches, others operate as self-styled ambassadors and merchants, sowing discord with far-ranging plots. While the inscrutable denizens dress themselves in flowing robes, veils, and broad turbans to appear human, their aberrant physiology, when glimpsed, is evident from their crown of stubby horns and tentacled jaws to their furry, goat-like legs. Denizens of Leng are often highly intelligent and can be found advising or nudging both the powerful and the meek alike. If there is any purpose to this beyond an individual Denizen of Leng's own interests, there has so far been no sign. Most appear to be perfectly genuine with their counsel, though generally lacking in anything resembling morality. Above the fierce captains who command the infamous black ships is an even higher caste of denizens of Leng. These occultists and musicians serve as high priests, laboring to appease the frightening gods who look down upon the Nightmare Realm with cold malice. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Denizen Of Leng](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4323) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 96 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +19, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +18, [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +17 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=413)_, Leng ruby worth 30 gp --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 100 (planar fast healing 5) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Resistances** critical hits 10, precision 10 **No Breath** Denizens of Leng don't need to breathe.
**Planar Fast Healing** A denizen of Leng maintains a connection to Leng at all times, and when away from Leng, they have [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5. They lose this ability in Leng or in areas where planar connections do not function. If killed, their body dissolves into nothingness in 1d4 rounds, leaving behind their equipment. A slain denizen reforms in Leng; they can be permanently killed only when their planar fast healing doesn't function. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _kukri_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d6+6 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Melee** jaws +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+6 piercing plus debilitating bite **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) - **3rd** [Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) - **4th** [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Debilitating Bite** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A creature that takes damage from a denizen's bite must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1. Each time a target fails an additional save against this ability, the condition value increases by 1 (to a maximum of clumsy 4). This condition value decreases by 1 every 24 hours. **Leng Ruby** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Many denizens of Leng carry strange rubies mined from quarries in Leng. As long as a creature holds a Leng ruby that it willingly accepted as a gift or payment from a denizen of Leng, any denizen of Leng can target that creature with [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602), [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), or [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) at a range of 1 mile, and the bearer uses an outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its saving throw against outcast's curse.
Leng Known variously as the Nightmare Realm or the Terror Beyond Dreams, the frozen Plateau of Leng looms physically and spiritually above the [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=47). Both a part of that dimension and a corruption of it, Leng is a realm inhabited by eldritch horrors and ruled by the horrors' ancient gods.
name: Denizen Of Leng

markdown:
Before the Jaathoom Empire, the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29) was ruled by destiny tempests from their Reverient Empire of Lost Nights. When the jaathoom armies crushed the Reverient Empire, they trapped the defeated destiny tempests in bronze spheres scattered across the Plane of Air, prisons of time and everlasting nightmare. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Destiny Tempest](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4324) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 97 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +26, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +8 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +24 **Will** +26 **HP** 230 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) 10, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 10 **Ebbing Cloud** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 15 feet. Destiny tempests surround themselves with thoughts of averted fates, creating a thick metaphysical soup that cloys the mind and clouds ambition. Creatures in the area moving toward the destiny tempest treat the area as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
**No Breath** Destiny tempests do not need to breathe.
**Unspeakable Insights** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Touching a destiny tempest's mind even briefly grants a powerful and painful awareness of uncharted pasts, presents, and futures, too impossibly vast for mortal minds to conceptualize or contain. Whenever a creature targets the destiny tempest with a magical mental effect, it must attempt a DC 33 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to unspeakable insights for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes 3d6 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d6 mental damage and becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Center of Destiny** ([teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet benefits from a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect; **Effect** Shadows surround and steal away the destiny tempest, who reappears in an open space adjacent to the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet; swiftness **Melee** slithering whisper +25 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+10 bludgeoning plus 1d8 mental and sound without voice **Ranged** umbral breath +25 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 4d10 void **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) - **5th** [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) - **6th** [Never Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Divergent Potential** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) The destiny tempest chooses two creatures it can see within 60 feet and rolls two slithering whisper Strikes, one against each creature. After seeing the outcomes of the two Strikes, the destiny tempest chooses one of the two targets to pursue, [Flies](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) up to 60 feet to reach the chosen target, and uses the result of the chosen Strike; the other Strike is lost. If the destiny tempest is prevented from reaching its chosen target, the attack is prevented and the chosen Strike is lost. **Sound Without Voice** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A creature damaged by the destiny tempest's slithering whisper Strike must succeed at a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save or become [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). **Swiftness** A destiny tempest's movement doesn't trigger reactions.
Hushed Voices On the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29), many creatures communicate through telepathy, shared dreams, or illusory rebuses. Though destiny tempests are telepathic and can magically understand most languages, they prefer to convey their thoughts and ideas metaphorically by shaping the clouds and currents of the Plane of Air to act out elaborate, moving stories and plays. Sometimes these animated clouds retain a bit of magic, becoming [Pictures-in-Clouds](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2619).
name: Destiny Tempest

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Zebub (Accuser Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4325)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Zebubs serve as [Hell's](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) messengers and spies. Their ability to share what they've seen with other creatures makes them especially useful—not only to other devils but also to mortal conjurers. Some infernal lords unleash them in enormous, horrid swarms upon unsuspecting lands to debase flesh and land alike while collecting secrets the infernal host might later put to use. Zebubs use any opportunity to manipulate weak-willed or easily tempted mortals into serving the zebubs' whims. 

While arrogant and deceitful, zebubs lack the cunning and confidence of most devils, and thus their schemes often focus on satisfying self-serving or self-destructive ambitions. Zebubs form from the souls of childish and craven mortals, reshaped by the archdevil [Baalzebul](/Deities.aspx?ID=394) in the frozen, filthy wastes of Hell's seventh layer, Cocytus.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Devil_Zebub.png" />
</column>

</row>

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +8, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10

</row>

</column>

+1 status to all saves vs. magic

[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)

physical 5 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5­­

</column>

<actions string="Single Action" />
mandibles +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 1d10+5 piercing plus Cocytan filth

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
- **2nd**

**Diabolic Eye** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The zebub records everything they see, and though they don't remember all observations, they can pass them along to another creature. The zebub replays 10 minutes of witnessed events to a touched willing creature, which receives the memories in a flash of information. By remaining in contact, the zebub can spend additional 3-action activities to replay more information. After relaying their visions to another, the zebub can't ever recall those events again.

**Sneak Attack**   The zebub's Strikes deal an additional 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

Fearsome giants of jagged iron, levalochs serve in the armies of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) as potent warriors and tenacious hunters—creatures of absolute discipline endlessly obedient to diabolical tyrants. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 23 **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Levaloch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4327) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 99 **Perception** +16; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +14 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Hellstrider** A levaloch ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain. They take no damage from caltrops or from damaging terrain that deals physical, acid, or cold damage. A levaloch can move through liquids up to 5 feet deep at their full Speed. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +12 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 105 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Phalanx Fighter** All devils of equal or lower level adjacent to a levaloch gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their AC as the levaloch shields them from harm.
**Stable Stance** A levaloch gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude DC against being [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) and to other saving throws to resist being moved against their will. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** _trident_ +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus merciless thrust **Ranged** _trident_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing **Ranged** _barbed net_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** barbed net **Rituals** DC 22 - **1st** [Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) **Barbed Net** When a levaloch hits a creature with their barbed net, the net wraps around the target, which becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds. If the Strike was a critical success, the target is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81). When a creature [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 22), or if the Strike misses, the net crumbles into rust. Each time a creature attempts to Escape, it takes 1d6 slashing damage from the net's barbs, regardless of whether the attempt succeeds. **Forge Weapon** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) A levaloch reforges part of their barbed iron substance into a new _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401)_ or barbed net. Their previous trident crumbles to rust. When the levaloch is destroyed, any tridents or barbed nets they created crumble to rust. **Merciless Thrust** When a levaloch hits a creature that has the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) conditions with a melee trident Strike, the Strike deals an additional 2d6 damage.
Hellforged Construct Levalochs are strange amalgamations of devil and automaton, never requiring food or rest. The engravings on their iron plates indicate their hellish allegiance to a certain [archdevil](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=4), [infernal duke](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=28), malebranche, or [Queen of the Night](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=14).
name: Levaloch
