markdown:
These ram-headed devils have wings and flesh made of rusted metal. They're forged from the souls of damned mortals who made others suffer through their creative work. They like to tempt those of the same ilk, driving auteurs and stage directors to cruelty in the pursuit of greatness. Ferrugons also enjoy pushing artists to extremes in order to break them. They dip the bodies of mortals they've driven to despair into molten metal to make horrifically malformed statues, which they then add to their lairs. Rust devils prefer temptation to fighting and only enter combat if protecting something dear to them. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Ferrugon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4328) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 100 **Perception** +22; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +25 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +20 **Will** +21 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 190 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **Metallic** A ferrugon is a metallic creature and thus affected by effects such as the circumstance penalty inflicted by [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721).
**Vainglorious Whispers** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A non-[devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) creature within 30 feet of the ferrugon succeeds (but doesn't critically succeed) at an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw; **Effect** The ferrugon whispers subversive messages to the triggering creature, causing it to become overly confident in its abilities, while in fact it becomes less accomplished overall. The target must attempt a DC 32 Will save. On a failure, the target gains a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects but also takes a –2 penalty to all attack rolls and skill checks for 1 hour. During this time, the victim can't benefit from [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) reactions, use [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effects on themself, or use [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) or [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) actions, as these actions seem unnecessary to the creature at this time. Similar defensive actions might not be available to the victim as well, at the GM's discretion. The target is then temporarily immune to Vainglorious Whispers for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** horn +25 ([cold iron](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [cold iron](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d4+16 slashing plus ferrugon tetanus **Ranged** iron feather +23 ([cold iron](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d4+13 piercing plus ferrugon tetanus **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **5th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751) (×3; wall is made of rusty iron; not stone) - **6th** [Petrify](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628) (target is transformed into rusty iron; not stone) **Rituals** DC 32 - **1st** [Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) **Ferrugon Tetanus** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; Onset 1d4 days; **Stage 1** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 week); **Stage 2** clumsy 2 and can't speak (1 day); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) (1 day); **Stage 4** death **Sunder Objects** When a ferrugon damages an item or structure, they deal an additional 2d8 damage to that item or structure.
Makers Of Rust A ferrugon’s [_petrify_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628) and [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751) [innate spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) result in rusted iron objects instead of stone. Since this iron is rusted and flawed, it shares the same physical statistics as the [stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2359) created by the spells and is too low-quality to serve as a source for forging metal objects.
name: Ferrugon

markdown:
Where a bright mind seeking knowledge becomes corrupted by eloquent lies and twisted truths, an ayngavhaul is sure to lurk. Often donned in the holy vestments of other faiths in a twisted mockery of their principles, these wily, well-read devils delight in using their knowledge to twist religious texts and teachings into the dangerous principles those same texts warn against. While many ayngavhauls develop advanced knowledge of unique specializations, there are many whose depth of knowledge of a single religion rivals that of even the most wizened priests. Any information an ayngavhaul has read or learned is added to a massive living tome unique to the ayngavhaul. The information contained sorts itself based on the topic and point its owner is making. While these devils spend most of their time in the libraries of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), they are most often summoned in desperate bids by students seeking lost or forbidden knowledge, and such knowledge comes with a cost. These devils use truths and loose interpretations of texts to lend credence to their heretical viewpoints. When these once-bright intellectuals are corrupted into blasphemous priests, tyrants, and despots spreading the twisted words of an ayngavhaul, that devil gains influence and renown within the scholarly circles of Hell. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Ayngavhaul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4329) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 101 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +26, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +24, [Library Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +24 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +8 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Personal Library** Any tomes an ayngavhaul is reading or referencing for their current work can be stored in the devil's personal library, a floating collecting of tomes revolving around the devil that can be used offensively or defensively. Retrieving or returning a tome requires an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259) action. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +20 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 240 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **Spellblock Tome** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** The ayngavhaul is targeted by a spell; **Effect** The ayngavhaul flings a tome from its personal library at the spell. The devil must attempt a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, the tome fully absorbs the effects of the spell and burns up into a harmless pile of ash. Regardless of the result, the devil can't use this ability again for 1d4 rounds. --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+8 slashing **Ranged** searing words +27 ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d10+8 mental plus 1d6 fire and poison minds **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Phase Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=2576), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Never Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614) **Herald Heresy** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The ayngavhaul imparts blasphemous thoughts into the minds of all non-devil creatures within a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) up to 60 feet away. An affected creature takes 2d10 mental damage plus 2d10 spirit damage and must attempt a DC 33 Will save. Affected creatures gain a cumulative +1 circumstance bonus (up to a total of +4) to saves against all future attempts to Herald Heresy for 1 minute, as they become inured to the blasphemies.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and becomes slowed 2. **Poison Minds** Creatures hit by the ayngavhaul's searing words must succeed at a DC 33 Will saving throw or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round (or stupefied 2 on a critical failure). If the target is trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), they take a –2 circumstance penalty to their save.
Beyond Peer Review Each ayngavhaul carries a personal tome that is an ever-changing encyclopedia of knowledge and literature laced with malevolent bias and half-truths. When an ayngavhaul reads their personal tome, they can intrinsically understand the concepts presented and the true nature of the text, which is often needed to add credibility to their arguments. Depending on the personality of the ayngavhaul, they will sometimes take on a tome they find as their new personal tome, transfixed by the new truths found within. The tome is illegible to all other creatures.
name: Ayngavhaul

markdown:
As masters of cold, calculated logic and perfectly timed proselytization, the loathsome deimaviggas seek to set friend against friend and turn the faithful from their beliefs at every opportunity. Their most common tools are mortal ego and despair. Those who are high-ranking or ambitious among their faith are lured toward self-aggrandizement until they see themselves first and their deity and clergy second. Those prone to doubt or heartache are isolated, as the deimavigga disrupts their [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spells and weakens their faith. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Deimavigga](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4330) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 102 **Perception** +32; (36 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); indomitable oration, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +33 **Str** +7 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +9 **Indomitable Oration** Any creature capable of comprehending speech understands the deimavigga, as if they constantly spoke in all languages at once. **Items** _[+2 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [raiment ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2791) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_ --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +29 **Will** +32 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 285 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Whispers of Discord** **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet is targeted by a spell that would restore Hit Points or provide a status bonus (the deimavigga automatically recognizes such effects); **Effect** The deimavigga whispers disturbing lies, audible only to the target, to shake the target's faith in the spell's caster. The target must attempt a DC 38 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target disbelieves the lies and receives the intended benefit of the spell; the target becomes temporarily immune to Whispers of Discord for 24 hours.
**Success** As critical success, but the target isn't temporarily immune.
**Failure** The spell fails to affect the target. The target refuses all [aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) from that caster for 1 round and doesn't count as the caster's ally.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration is 1 minute. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+18 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +34 - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503) (at will) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **7th** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) - **9th** [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570) (at will) **Rituals** DC 43 - **1st** [Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) **Boundless Reach** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) A deimavigga's razor-sharp claws can slice through reality, allowing them to make claw Strikes and use spells with a range of touch against any creature they can see directly or via magic. A creature targeted in this way can retaliate until the start of the deimavigga's next turn; it can target the devil's claws as if the devil were physically present and adjacent to the target, though the claws are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The deimavigga can take on the appearance of any humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or attack and damage bonuses with Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Whisper Earworm** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The deimavigga whispers a terrifying multiversal truth to one adjacent creature, shaking its faith in reality and divinity. The target must attempt a DC 38 Will save. [Celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) and [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) gain a +2 status bonus to this save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and temporarily immune to Whisper Earworm for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The next time the target rests, it ruminates on the deimavigga's words instead of sleeping or otherwise resting and awakens fatigued. The target also becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until it's no longer fatigued.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but drained 2. After this rest, the target must attempt another DC 38 Will save. On a failure, the target becomes stupefied 2 and takes a –4 status penalty to Will saves against effects from unholy creatures. These effects last until the target unlearns the truth spoken by the deimavigga, requiring a rewrite memory spell, other means of modifying their memory, or powerful magic such as a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual.
Lore Operators While most devils perform a specialized role within a complex infernal machine, deimaviggas work almost exclusively alone, as they find even the most obedient minions a hindrance to their stratagems.
name: Deimavigga

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dibrasgorth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4331)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dibrasgorths, sometimes called Mothers of Oblivion, are monstrous creatures of chaos who dwell in lightless spaces, often near the bottom of deep lakes and oceans both above and underground. They may look like hideous monsters with heads like a plesiosaur atop masses of tentacles, each tipped with a baleful red eye, but dibrasgorths have a twisted sense of cunning, likely due to their ability to see into and affect planes other than the one where they stand. Though they are quite powerful (with some the favored servants of Lamashtu), dibrasgorths prefer to keep their existence a secret from the mortals of the world above.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mother_Of_Oblivion.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Dibrasgorth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4331)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=529" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 104

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) 120 feet, planar sight 120 feet, [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +1

**Con** +4

**Int** +5

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Planar Sight** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The eyes at the end of their tentacles allow a dibrasgorth to see into planes coterminous with the one it is currently on at the listed range. For instance, if they're in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), they can see into the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) and [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49)

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +23 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 250

</row>

**Immunities**
[death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [petrification](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)

**Resistances**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 15, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 15

**Warped Space** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 100 feet. The dibrasgorth's presence distorts the fabric of space. Any other creature who uses a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effect or spell within the aura must attempt a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 piercing plus draining bite

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 33
- **Cantrips (7th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
- **4th**
[Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
- **5th**
[Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1650)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576)
- **Constant (5th)**
[See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)

**Breath of Phantasms** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The dibrasgorth exhales a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of noxious gas. Each creature in the area takes 7d6 poison damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (or 2 rounds on a critical failure).

**Drag Through Dimensions** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The dibrasgorth has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) with a tentacle; **Effect** The dibrasgorth's tentacle whips through coterminous planes as it smashes the creature it is holding against the ground and other natural features in each plane before returning to this plane. The creature takes 5d8 bludgeoning damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who fails the save is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the rapid planar travel.

**Draining Bite**  ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A dibrasgorth feeds on the spirits of its victims. A creature that is damaged by the dibrasgorth's jaws Strike must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (drained 2 on a critical failure). In addition, the dibrasgorth gains 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute if the creature fails or critically fails the save.

**Transdimensional Tentacles**  ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dibrasgorth can worm its tentacles through nearby planes to attack. While in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), its tentacle Strikes ignore all [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) from objects unless those objects exist on both the Universe and either [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) or the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34), or the objects have the [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596) trait.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Myth Of Black Magga</title>

Varisian locals who live near the Storval Deep, an enormous freshwater lake on the Storval Plateau, tell tales of Black Magga, a powerful and unholy dibrasgorth rumored to be older than the gods. They say that terrible storms presage her appearance near the lake's surface, and that those who see her form and live are cursed to be unable to completely describe her, with black blood welling in their mouth if they make the attempt.
</aside>
name: Dibrasgorth

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dig-Widget](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4332)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Thieves covet dig-widgets, specialized constructs built for infiltration. Each dig-widget contains numerous simple tools, including a set of mechanical devices that function as [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758), two arms with drills, and two arms with corkscrews for attaching to and climbing surfaces. Once activated, these devices propel themselves forward. Though they have the faculties typical of a construct, they usually follow a simple routine: avoid notice, pick any lock barring the path, dig past obstacles, and attack if caught. They're rarely left unattended, as a thief needs to be nearby to follow after—both to steal goods and to stop the dig-widget from engaging in further larceny once it's achieved its goal.

The source of a dig-widget's power is as much mechanical as it is magical. The gears and springs that provide a dig-widget mobility are an improvement over more primitive true clockwork creations (whose functions require constant winding to remain mobile), but at the cost of security, for a digwidget's moving parts can be dismantled quickly by thieves and others with the proper training.

While many authorities ban dig-widgets, they have a harder time confiscating the machines than might be expected. A sizable number of people who encounter dig-widgets, from hired adventurers to tunneling kobolds, are far more inclined to keep the constructs for themselves than turn them over to be dismantled. Often, this is simply due to the novelty of the dig-widget rather than any desire to use it.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/DigWidget.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Dig-Widget](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4332)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 105

**Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +1

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Infiltration Tools** A dig-widget's face consists of an [infiltrator thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758). This toolkit can be salvaged from a destroyed dig-widget with a successful DC 20 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check. On a failed check, the tools are destroyed.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=60), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Mechanical Vulnerability** A creature with expert proficiency in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) can attempt a check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) to damage a dig-widget. The DC is 22, and each success deals 20 damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
drill +14 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
corkscrew +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 piercing

**Fastening Leap** <actions string="Single Action" />  The dig-widget [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up 20 feet onto a creature or object and attempts a corkscrew [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against it. If the Strike damages the target, the dig-widget attaches to the target (typically to the back of a creature). This is similar to [Grabbing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the creature, but the dig-widget moves with that creature rather than holding it in place. While attached, the dig-widget can't use its corkscrew. The dig-widget can be [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) off, or it can detach itself with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action.

**Sneak Attack**   A dig-widget's Strikes deal an additional 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Widget Workshops</title>

The first dig-widget came from the workshops of a dwarven thieves' guild called Godak's Grifters, which used more advanced magical clockwork theories as a springboard for these simpler but no less effective contraptions. With their dig-widgets, the Grifters plagued the authorities of several dwarven settlements over the years. Their travels spread dig-widget technology, and numerous improvements have since led to faster and more reliable versions. Though upstanding dwarven mechanics have observed dig-widgets and recognized the complexity of the technology, they've steadfastly refused to adapt something with such an unscrupulous origin.
</aside>
name: Dig-Widget

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Compsognathus Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4333)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Though a curious lone compsognathus is occasionally known to sneak into an adventurer's camp, they're often scouting from a nearby pack potentially containing dozens of the tiny bipedal dinosaurs. If the pack is threatened— especially near a nesting site—they'll scurry about in self-defense. Despite their large numbers, the swarm can dart away from danger quickly, and their gnashing teeth carry the threat of venom to anyone who comes too close.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Dinosaur_Compsognathus_Swarm.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Dinosaur" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=576" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=707" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 106

**Perception** +9; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40

</row>

**Immunities**
[grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), precision, [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78)

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 2, piercing 5, slashing 5

**Weaknesses**
area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5

**Evade** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An adjacent enemy targets the swarm with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167); **Effect** With quick movements, the swarm gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, the swarm can [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to 10 feet after the Strike.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, swim 15 feet

**Venomous Bites** <actions string="Single Action" />  Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 2d4 piercing damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who fails the save is also exposed to compsognathus venom.

**Compsognathus Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d8 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Dinosaur Swarms</title>

Because they all depend on laying eggs to reproduce, dinosaurs generally have nesting areas of which they're fiercely protective. While larger dinosaurs like allosauruses are slightly more prepared to take on a threat alone, smaller species often rely on safety in numbers to protect their homes. Once a threat is posed to their eggs, these diminutive creatures seem to form a cloud of frenzied jaws and claws, all moving together as if sharing one mind.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-391" />
name: Compsognathus Swarm

markdown:
Velociraptors are clever pack hunters who generally subsist off small herbivores but are not afraid to take on larger prey as a group. Bigger populations of velociraptors are more dangerous when they coalesce into a swarm to protect their young. Even in large numbers, these feathered raptors remain uncannily nimble and can lunge suddenly at their enemies. Emboldened by their increased numbers, the swarms will lash out fiercely with their talons and doggedly pursue any perceived threat. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Velociraptor Pack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4334) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 106 **Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +14 **Will** +8 **HP** 75 **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Bites and Talons** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The pack makes a melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 piercing or slashing damage and 1d4 precision damage
2d6 piercing or slashing damage and 2d4 precision damage
3d6 piercing or slashing damage and 2d4 precision damage **Puff Up** The velociraptors ruffle their plumage to appear larger. They ignore the –4 penalty to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) for not knowing the same language as their target. **Raptor Leap** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The pack [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), [ignoring difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) (but not greater difficult terrain). At the end of this movement, each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 1d8 piercing or slashing damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
name: Velociraptor Pack

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Iguanodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4335)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Iguanodons are large, herbivorous dinosaurs that inhabit swamps and forests where they feed on the abundant vegetation. The iguanodon is capable of moving on two feet or on four, quickly switching from one stance to the other depending on whether it needs to move through dense foliage or to reach delectable morsels hanging up in the canopy.

Although iguanodons are herbivores, they're notoriously quick to anger. Their thumb spikes make their claws particularly devastating weapons; a well-placed blow from one of these claws can turn a hungry predator into a cowering beast with one swift strike. Iguanodons are 30 feet long and weigh 6,000 pounds.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Allosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4336)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The allosaurus is a huge bipedal dinosaur built for hunting larger prey. Their legs are long and powerful, and their specially hinged jaw allows their mouth to open wide enough to attack creatures who tower over them and to gulp down smaller creatures with frightening ease. Their short arms are tipped in razor-sharp claws, and their thick tails can provide a powerful sweeping defense in a pinch. Occasionally, allosauruses live and hunt in small groups, working together to take down especially large prey. However, they prefer staying in their own territory, reigning over their prey within. Some of their known prey include the [brontosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2922), [stegosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2920), or whole livestock if an allosaurus strays too close to unprotected farmlands.

The allosaurus [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to its Speed toward the required creature to an unoccupied space within reach of its tail Strike. The allosaurus makes a tail Strike against the enemy. If the Strike hits, the allosaurus automatically succeeds at a free action to [Knockdown](/Actions.aspx?ID=21) the target.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** <actions string="Single Action" />  Large, 2d6+3 bludgeoning, Rupture 15

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Allosaurus Teeth</title>

Allosauruses have serrated teeth, allowing them to more easily slash the flesh of large or slippery prey. These teeth are valuable not only because of their rarity but also because of their multiple practical uses. Larger allosaurus teeth can be made into arrow or spear tips, small knives, or worn as jewelry to indicate social status. While some cultures revere these artifacts, the killing of allosauruses for the express purpose of harvesting their teeth is outlawed in many regions across Golarion.
</aside>

markdown:
The spinosaurus is more than just one of the largest carnivorous dinosaurs—it's also one of the most unusual in appearance, with a large, sail-like fin running along its spine. Often quite colorful, this sail allows the spinosaurus to attract mates, aids in swimming, and makes it appear to be even larger than it actually is. A swimming spinosaurus can also use the sail as part of a unique means of staggering prey by slapping the water with it to make a crushing wave. A spinosaurus can measure up to 60 feet in length and weigh 25,000 pounds. The spinosaurus is equally at home in water as it is on land, and its long, toothy maw is well-adapted for catching swimming prey. Attempts by giants to capture spinosauruses to serve as guardians typically go poorly, for these headstrong dinosaurs do not domesticate well. Their surly attitudes and striking appearances make them better suited for blood sports, and they're popular prizes for those who run arenas specializing in battles that pit gladiators against hungry animals or beasts. These productions are truly feasts of gore for the eyes and command audiences from hundreds of miles around. Of course, an angry dinosaur forced to fight for the amusement of others won't discriminate between potential meals on the battleground and those seated in the surrounding stands, making the seats closest to the edge of the arena possibly part of the show as well. The spinosaurus's appearance and strength make it attractive to more than just giants and blood sport organizers. Spellcasters who mutate and transform animals into magical guardians have long been intrigued by the spinosaurus's potential. Due to the creature's reputation for violence, these spellcasters face great danger while charming one to bring home. Yet for those intrigued by the creature's hypothetical abilities, such risk is worth it. More so than any other dinosaur, spinosauruses have been subjected to fleshwarping procedures, crossbreeding with monsters, and other magical techniques at the hands of reclusive spellcasters experimenting to enhance the creatures' viability as effective guardians. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Spinosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4337) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 108 **Perception** +21; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Deep Breath** A spinosaurus can hold its breath for 2 hours. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 **HP** 200 --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +23 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+14 slashing **Rip and Tear** **Requirements** The spinosaurus has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or restrained in its jaws; **Effect** The spinosaurus reaches up and slashes with its claws at the creature it has grabbed, dealing 4d8 slashing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who fails this save also takes 1d6 p[ersistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). **Staggering Sail** ([incapacitation **Requirements** The spinosaurus is swimming on the surface of water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Effect** With a powerful lunge to the side, the spinosaurus uses its sail to slap the surface of the water, creating a crushing wave of water that deals 6d6 bludgeoning damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature in the water in the area must attempt a DC 30 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Medium, 2d12+6 bludgeoning, Rupture 19
name: Spinosaurus

This divine warden serves [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), the goddess of birth, death, and fate. It protects a temple or shrine to the Lady of Graves. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Divine Warden Of Pharasma](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4338) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 115 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** -2 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +14 **HP** 95 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Divine Destruction** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) When the divine warden is reduced to 0 HP, it erupts with divine energy in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), dealing 7d6 spirit damage per level. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 21 Will save with the following outcomes.
**Critical Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Success** The creature takes full damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes temporarily [cursed](/Traits.aspx?ID=567) by the patron deity. The creature becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 day; this is a curse effect that uses the Will save DC as the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature becomes enfeebled 2 and stupefied 2.
**Faith Bound** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A divine warden can't attack a creature that openly wears or displays the religious symbol of the divine warden's patron deity unless that creature uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against the divine warden first. If the divine warden is intelligent, it can also attack a creature it believes isn't faithful to its deity or who wears the religious symbol as a ruse (typically after succeeding at a [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** dagger +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+7 piercing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21, attack +13 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498) **Divine Domain Spells,** DC 21 1 Focus Point - **3rd** [Death's Call](/Spells.aspx?ID=1792), [Healer's Blessing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1808) **Faithful Weapon** A divine warden always wields its patron deity's favored weapon. If the weapon is a ranged weapon, the divine warden automatically generates new ammunition with each attack. For a divine warden of 4th level or higher, the deity's favored weapon gains the effects of a [_striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) rune while the divine warden wields it. The Divine Warden of Pharasma wields a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) with a _striking_ rune. **Instrument of Faith** The divine warden is a beacon for its deity's faith. A [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=33) of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) can channel a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell through the divine warden they can see within 60 feet. The cleric determines any targets or area for the spell as if they were standing in the divine warden's space. **Mask of Fate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) The divine warden of Pharasma peers at a single creature within 60 feet through the eyes of its mask to alter its destiny. The target must attempt a DC 21 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes a –1 status penalty to the next saving throw it attempts within the next minute against a [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) effect from a divine warden of Pharasma or worshipper of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290).
**Failure** For the next saving throw the target attempts within the next minute against a divine effect from a divine warden of Pharasma or worshipper of Pharasma, it rolls twice and takes the worse result.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect applies to all applicable saving throws.
name: Divine Warden Of Pharasma

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Doru](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4339)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These deceivers whisper fetid lies, sowing dark and dread inspiration into foolish mortal ears. Dorus serve as the spies and messengers of the divs. With silver tongues and wicked motives, they often pledge themselves to vainglorious and deluded spellcasters, whom they ply with wild conspiracies and rumors of deception among friends and foes alike; in the process, they push supposed masters to despotism and murderous revenge. All the while, dorus wean secrets and weaknesses from their marks. In the end, nearly every doru turns on their supposed liege, destroying the reputation and even causing the death of the person the doru pretended to serve.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Div_Doru.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Doru](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4339)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Div" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=351" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 110

**Perception** +7; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (touch)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +6, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 3, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 3

**Covetous of Secrets** Dorus have a weakness for secrets, hoarding them like a miser hoards gold. A creature can tempt a doru with some bit of obscure knowledge the doru doesn't know or thinks they don't know. Presenting the hint of the secret is a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) and [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) traits, and requires a skill check using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) (or some other appropriate skill determined by the GM) against the doru's Will DC. On a success, the doru is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) for as long as the presenter draws out the explanation of the secret (spending 1 action each round doing so, to a maximum of 1 minute). On a critical success, the doru is fascinated for that duration plus 1 minute more as they ponder the implications of the secret. Regardless of the outcome, the doru is temporarily immune to that creature's attempts to present them with secrets for 1 day.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing plus doru venom

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only)
- **4th**
[Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647)

**Doru Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d6 poison and stupefied 2 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Doru Secrets</title>

Despite dorus' obsession, not all secrets capture their focus—instead, each fixates on a unique topic. Many are interested in the history of a particular mortal ancestry, while others delve into riddles, mathematical puzzles, or even local gossip.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-580" />
name: Doru

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />

markdown:
Walking embodiments of curses, misfortune, and the evil eye, aghashes wander the deserts of [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), searching out the beauty of the world to destroy, humiliate, and undermine. Aghashes are often mistaken for some strange form of [hag](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=425), a misunderstanding that they're content to encourage, and like those creatures, they're masterful at curses. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Aghash](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4340) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 111 **Perception** +12; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +10 **Will** +12 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 75 **Immunities** [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Hatred of Art** While aghashes hate all mortals, they particularly despise beautiful objects and artistic mortals. When not in physical peril, an aghash is compelled to destroy art and other works of beauty. An aghash can't enter an area of pristine beauty without first marring it in some way. Given a choice, an aghash will attack a foe who is an obvious crafter or performer of some kind. A [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32) casting a composition spell, a runesmith tracing a rune, a street magician performing a daring escape, and similar abilities as determined by the GM draw the aghash's ire. If the aghash is barred from attacking such foes, either by force or some magical effect, they take 1d6 mental damage at the end of their turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+5 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **1st** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (at will) - **2nd** [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) (at will) - **4th** [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Rituals** DC 21 - **1st** [Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46) **Cursed Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The aghash fixes their gaze on one creature they can see within 20 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 21 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 mental damage and becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6 mental damage and becomes either frightened 2 or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (the aghash's choice).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 8d6 mental damage and becomes frightened 2 and stunned 2. **Sandstorm** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The aghash creates a temporary sandstorm in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that lasts for 1 minute. Creatures within the emanation take a –4 circumstance penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save. On a failure, they're forced to hold their breath or else they start suffocating. A creature within the sandstorm at the end of its turn takes 1d6 slashing damage. Divs are immune to all effects of an aghash's Sandstorm.
name: Aghash

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pairaka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4341)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Often charming, sometimes even seductive, pairakas worm their way into mortal relationships, subtly destroying all ties of friendship and love through emotional and physical manipulation and corruption. Their natural form is not often seen. Instead, they adopt shapes that attract just enough attention to earn the trust of those they wish to torment and corrupt. When they do show a more natural form, pairakas typically choose to feed into the belief of their beauty by appearing as androgynous, pale blue, aesthetically pleasing humanoids who carry themselves gracefully wherever they go. 

While this form is only a part of their manipulative tactics, their intrinsic form is hidden to almost all except other pairakas. They're frightful, towering creatures, spotted with large rashes and boils. With horns that grow and twist with age, and a smile that bares razor-sharp teeth, this form is both shocking and unsettling. Pairakas may occasionally show their true form to those they've preyed upon, but only once they've been completely corrupted, with all their other relationships in ruins.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Div_Pairaka.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Pairaka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4341)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Div" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=351" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 112

**Perception** +15; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +17 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 105

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5

**Hatred of Red** Pairakas hate the color red. They won't wear the color or willingly enter any place painted in a shade of red. Given a choice, they'll attack a creature wearing red before others, seeing their choice to do so as a personal affront. If barred from expressing their displeasure toward the color by force or some magical effect, they take 2d6 mental damage at the end of their turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus bubonic plague

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 25
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will)

**Rituals** DC 25

- **1st**
[Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46)

**Bubonic Plague**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) A creature can't remove the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition while infected; **Saving Throw** DC 23 Fortitude; Onset 1 day; **Stage 1** fatigued (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 3, fatigued, and takes 1d6 persistent bleed damage every 1d20 minutes (1 day)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The pairaka can take the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid or animal. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their strikes deal.

**Tormenting Dreams** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The pairaka torments a sleeping creature within 100 feet with visions of betrayals by loved ones and friends. The target must attempt a DC 25 Will save, with the effects of the [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) spell.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-580" />
name: Pairaka

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Drake Courser](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1479)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=50" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 13

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet, snow vision

**Languages**
[Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +7

**Con** +5

**Int** -1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Snow Vision** Snow doesn't impair a drake courser's vision. It ignores [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from snowfall.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +18 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 230

</row>

**Immunities**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15

**Attack of Opportunity** <actions string="Reaction" /> Tail only. On a critical hit, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31); if the target was using a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, its movement ends.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet, fly 65 feet, swim 35 feet, ice climb 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +25,
**Damage** 3d12+11 piercing plus 1d6 cold

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +25 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20)

**Battle Dance** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The drake courser makes two fangs Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. It can replace any of these Strikes with [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) or Step.

**Buffeting Surge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The drake courser moves up to twice its Speed. It can attempt to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) with its tail once at any point during its movement.

**Freezing Mist Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The drake spits a ball of liquid up to 60 feet that explodes into a 20-foot burst of freezing mist. Those in the burst take 13d6 cold damage (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The mist cakes all surfaces in the area with a sheet of slippery ice that turns the area into difficult terrain for 2d4 rounds. It can't use Freezing Mist Breath again for 1d6 rounds.

**Ice Climb**   A drake courser can climb on ice as though it had the listed climb Speed. It ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain from ice and snow and doesn't risk falling when crossing ice.

</column>
name: Drake Courser

[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Burning Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=30), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=122), [Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=167), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) (4 slots)

**Sorcerer Bloodline Spells** DC 35, 3 Focus Points
- **7th**
[Elemental Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=503) (air), [Elemental Motion](/Spells.aspx?ID=504) (air), [Elemental Toss](/Spells.aspx?ID=505) (air)

**Bludgeoning Energy**   The students' combine elemental energies with physical brawn, blasting opponents with huge gusts of compressed air instead of typical energies. Any of the students' spells that deal energy damage (such as [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [_burning hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=30), [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), and so on) instead deal bludgeoning damage.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Kas Xi Rai](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1481)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 46

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Kas Xi Rai](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1481)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 23

**Perception** +26

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Crime Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +31

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +6

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Items**
[_bracers of armor II_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416), _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, [_potion of undetectability_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=195)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 38 (42 vs. reactions and ranged weapon attacks)

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +32 

**Will** +29 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

**Tangled Forest Trip** <actions string="Free Action" /> **Requirements** Kas Xi Rai is in tangled forest stance; **Trigger** A creature leaves a space within Kas Xi Rai's reach; **Effect** The triggering creature must attempt a DC 37 Reflex save, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check, or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. On a failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for that action.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet, climb 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 3d6+12 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lashing branch +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 slashing plus 1d6 acid

**Monk Ki Spells** DC 38, 3 Focus Points
- **8th**
[Ki Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=485), [Ki Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=486), [Wind Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=490)

**Exhausting Flurry**   When Kas Xi Rai targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows and at least one hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or be [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and take a –5-foot status penalty to land Speed while enfeebled. On a critical failure, the creature is enfeebled 2 and takes a –10-foot status penalty to land Speed while enfeebled.

**Flurry of Blows** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) Kas Xi Rai makes two Strikes with her fist. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

**Tangled Forest Rake** <actions string="Single Action" />  Kas Xi Rai makes a lashing branch Strike. If she hits and deals damage, she forces the target to move 5 feet into a space within her reach.

**Tangled Forest Stance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=152)) Kas Xi Rai extends her arms and enters a deadly tree-like combat stance. As long as she is in this stance, Kas Xi Rai can use Tangled Forest Trip and Tangled Forest Rake and make lashing branch Strikes with her fists.

</column>
name: Kas Xi Rai

Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 23 **Perception** +25 **Languages** [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +26 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +24 **Will** +23 **HP** 250 --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet **Melee** dagger +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d4+13 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Ranged** dagger +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d4+13 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Lasting Debilitating Strike** **Trigger** The xun's Strike hits a [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creature and deals damage; **Effect** The xun applies one of the following debilitations, which lasts until the end of the xun's next turn or for 1 week on a critical hit.
  • **Debilitation** The target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds.
  • **Debilitation** The target becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13).
**Looming Threat** The master xun Steps or [Feints](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), then [Demoralizes](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The master xun deals an extra 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) opponents.

+1 status to all saves vs. [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)

**[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** <actions string="Reaction" /> If Tino's Retributive Strike against the triggering creature doesn't critically miss, that creature meets the requirements for Tino to use Defiant Shove until the end of his next turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 45 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_fist_ +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 3d8+10 bludgeoning

<actions string="Single Action" />
_temple sword_ +24 ([Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 slashing plus 1d6 fire

- **7th**
[Ki Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=484), [Ki Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=486), [Lay on Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=395)

**Defiant Shove** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** Tino used Retributive Strike on a creature since the start of his previous turn, attempted the Strike it permitted, and didn't critically miss; **Effect** Tino throws his whole body into a powerful push. He moves the target 5 feet in any direction and can then attempt to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) that creature. This is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451).

**Divine Smite**   If Tino hits with his Retributive Strike, the target takes 3 [persistent good damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Flurry of Blows** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) Tino makes two Strikes with his fist or with a [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=112) weapon. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

**Powerful Fists**   Tino's fist Strikes don't take penalties when making lethal attacks.

</column>
name: Tino Tung (Level 13)

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 26

**Perception** +26; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Kitsune](/Languages.aspx?ID=86), [Nagaji](/Languages.aspx?ID=87), [Tengu](/Languages.aspx?ID=47), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
_+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [darts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68)_ (5), _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, _+1 [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)_, [greater smokesticks](/Equipment.aspx?ID=135) (2)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +20 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 190

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_claw_ +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 3d4+11 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_dart_ +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d4+6 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foxfire +24 ([range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d4 electricity

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 33
- **Cantrips (7th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61)
- **1st**
[Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=266)
- **5th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Ji-yook transforms into her [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6) form. Using Change Shape counts as creating a disguise for the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) use of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5). Ji-yook can remain in her human form indefinitely, and she can shift back to her kitsune form by using this action again.

**Foxfire Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) Ji-yook makes a fist Strike. On a hit the target takes an additional 3d4 electricity damage and is [pushed back](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet. On a critical hit, the target is pushed back and then knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**Pinning Shot** <actions string="Single Action" />  Ji-yook expertly aims and flings a dart to peg an opponent to a nearby wall or floor. Ji-yook makes a dart Strike. If the attack hits, it deals no damage and instead pins the target to an adjacent surface. The target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and must spend an Interact action to attempt a DC 10 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to pull the dart free; it can't move from its space until it succeeds. If Ji-yook's dart Strike was a critical hit, the target must succeed at two separate Athletics checks to pull the dart free. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) while it remains immobilized in this way.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   Ji-yook Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>
name: Ji-yook (Level 13)

</column>

<column gap="tiny">
